title: Support Interactive Media Ads (IMA) in Flutter (Android & iOS)
body:
### Is there an existing issue for this?

- [X] I have searched the [existing issues](https://github.com/flutter/flutter/issues)
- [X] I have read the [guide to filing a bug](https://flutter.dev/docs/resources/bug-reports)

### Use case

Flutter app owners/developers want to monetize their video content with in-stream video ads in line with IAB's VAST/VMAP specifications. Google's [Interactive Media Ads SDK](https://developers.google.com/interactive-media-ads) is a popular client library choice for publishers to do so in line with industry standards. Currently, there is no officially supported Flutter IMA plugin.




### Proposal

Requesting official Flutter support for IMA SDKS on iOS & Android platforms. Initial request is to support the client side architecture of IMA SDK.

title: [two_dimensional_scrollables] - TableView is missing padding attribute
body:
### Is there an existing issue for this?

- [X] I have read the [guide to filing a bug](https://flutter.dev/docs/resources/bug-reports)

### Use case

I have a `TableView` in a screen with an app bar that does not have a straight edge. Wrapping the `TableView` with a padding widget causes artifacts with the scroll glow.

![Screenshot_20230911-203251](https://github.com/flutter/flutter/assets/48153628/22dde1ae-69fa-4df2-867d-25961c847536)

### Proposal

`TableView` from the two_dimensional_scrollables package should have a `padding` attribute like any other scrollable widget that allows to add padding around the content without shrinking the scrollable area.

@Piinks

title: Allow an embedded Flutter Web view to size itself
body:
Copied from internal bug b/298531093 I'm integrating an embedded Flutter Web into a Google product. One of its limitations is that the Flutter view can only size itself to fill its viewport contents and not vice-versa. For example, if I attach a Flutter view to this div:
Then the Flutter view will be (correctly) constrained to 100x100, but if I don't specify the div's dimensions:
The Flutter will assume that the viewport size is 0x0. The expected behavior, at least for me, would be for the viewport to take the size reported by the Flutter view. Right now I can sort of get away with having a fixed height and variable width, but I run into problems when the browser window is resized (see b/298531093 for details). For certain kinds of embedded views, especially small views with dynamic content, it is difficult and a poor user experience to give the content a fixed size before rendering. Ideally, it would be possible for Flutter to render and resize it's DOM element depending on the outcome. I'm not sure how this feature request interacts with the actual rendering mechanics or if that makes the layout much more expensive... Maybe the process could be something like: 1. Perform build/layout based within a large container that has some reasonable maximum size 2. Check the actual size of the content and resize the DOM element to match 3. Paint into the DOM element (ideally no more expensive than if we had known this size up front?)

  @override

      body: CustomScrollView(
        slivers: <Widget>[
          SliverAppBar(
            title: Text(widget.title),
            pinned: false,
            snap: false,
            floating: true,
          ),
...
```




### Proposal

![push](https://github.com/flutter/flutter/assets/57101477/c9f07eab-fe18-4cf2-b262-7fa0982d5145)

As shown above, AppBar should push the header.
The above operation was implemented with the https://pub.dev/packages/sliver_tools package.

title: Enforce (or at least warn for) the min supported Android version
body:
We currently don't have any enforcement of the minimum sdk that we support (as defined in our [supported platforms](https://docs.flutter.dev/reference/supported-platforms)). 

Despite [increasing the minimum from 16 to 19](https://github.com/flutter/flutter/issues/82000), a fresh flutter app edited to have minSdkVersion 15 provides no error or warning. We should consider enforcing the minimum, or at least providing a warning.

We could also maybe use this to provide a warning 1 stable cut ahead of when we drop support for a version range, though I'm not sure that noise would be desirable.

title: Provide ability to determine if text was autofilled vs manually entered.
Add  a new parameter to `SizeTransition` to control the cross axis size factor
TableView Scrollbar Adjustment for top Pinned Row
body:
### Is there an existing issue for this?

- [X] I have searched the [existing issues](https://github.com/flutter/flutter/issues)
- [X] I have read the [guide to filing a bug](https://flutter.dev/docs/resources/bug-reports)

### Use case

For a normal tableview, you would most likely use the top row as a pinned row, and the pinning of the row works amazing!  The only problem is that the scrollbar scroll's over that row, which isn't what I would expect from a normal table, I would expect it to not go over the top row if pinned, sort of like this example:

![pinned_row_scrollbar](https://github.com/flutter/flutter/assets/38195772/47dac92c-d1af-41ad-b7f1-bf1118ae91a8)

What i have to do now is something like this:

`data: ScrollbarThemeData(
            thumbColor: MaterialStateProperty.all(
                Theme.of(context).colorScheme.onBackground.withOpacity(0.5)),
            trackColor: MaterialStateProperty.all(Colors.transparent),
            crossAxisMargin: 0,
            mainAxisMargin: 50,
            minThumbLength: 30,
            thickness: MaterialStateProperty.all(7),
            radius: const Radius.circular(15),
          ),`
      
Which makes the scrollbar not scroll all the way to the bottom b/c with mainAxisMargin it does the padding on both the top and bottom.  So, now I have to create a whole custom scrollbar for a normally used feature in a table view.

### Proposal

My proposal would be to easily add the ability for the scrollbar to adjust to the top pinned row for normal use-case of table views.

title: [flutter_markdown] Add a way to change the default `MarkdownStyleSheet` down the widget tree
body:
### Is there an existing issue for this?

- [X] I have searched the [existing issues](https://github.com/flutter/flutter/issues)
- [X] I have read the [guide to filing a bug](https://flutter.dev/docs/resources/bug-reports)

### Use case

Sometimes it's helpful to change the theme for the entire subtree, like you can do with the `Theme`, `DefaultTextStyle` or `IconTheme` widgets. It would be helpful to have the opportunity to do the same for the styles of `MarkdownBody` (changing `MarkdownStyleSheet`).

### Proposal

```dart
DefaultMarkdownStyleSheet(
  style: MarkdownStyleSheet(
    p: TextStyle(color: Colors.orange)
  )
  child: Card(
    child: MarkdownBody(
      data: "My text" // this text is orange
    ),
  ),
);

title: Update base ref from main->master on PRs opened to flutter/flutter
body:
### Is there an existing issue for this?

- [X] I have searched the [existing infra issues](https://github.com/flutter/flutter/issues?q=is%3Aopen+is%3Aissue+label%3Ateam-infra)

### Type of Request

feature request

### Infrastructure Environment

Cocoon Scheduler

### What is happening?

It would be nice if cocoon automatically detected whether you are merging to the correct default branch, and if you aren't have it automatically change to the correct one. This recently was an issue in https://github.com/flutter/flutter/pull/135097, where the base branch being merged to was `main`, which resulted in not all of the targets being run. Once we changed the base to `master`, all of the targets ran per usual.

### Steps to reproduce

1. I create a fork in `flutter/flutter`
2. I make a change in my fork and commit it
3. I open a PR, with the base set to `main`.
4. (Feature request) Cocoon recognizes that I am trying to merge to `main` and changes the base to `master`.

### Expected results

The PR I created is merging to the default branch, `master`.

title: FormState validation method to return a Map of the validation status of children FormField(s)
Allow `Sliver`s to be zoomed more easily
Support multi-view rendering
body:
In order to make the Flutter engine's rendering system support multiple views, we need to change multiple components across the engine. The plan can be found in design doc [flutter.dev/go/multi-view-pipeline-and-rasterizer](http://flutter.dev/go/multi-view-pipeline-and-rasterizer). Items are listed below. The order of the items does not represent dependency, and some items can be developed in parallel.

- [x] **Item 1:** Eliminate violations of the rendering rule (https://github.com/flutter/engine/pull/45300)
- [x] **Item 2:** Make the pipeline support multiple views. The `Animator` should collect (instead of dispatching) the layer tree in `Render` and dispatch them at the end of `BeginFrame` (https://github.com/flutter/engine/pull/44473).
- [x] **Item 3:** Make the `Rasterizer`'s structure compatible with multiple views. (https://github.com/flutter/engine/pull/45512)
- [x] **Item 4:** Allow the `ExternalViewEmbedder` to handle multiple views. (https://github.com/flutter/engine/pull/51722, https://github.com/flutter/engine/pull/51719, https://github.com/flutter/engine/pull/46169)
- [x] **Abandoned:** Allow the `RasterCache` to handle multiple views. 
  - Per @/stuartmorgan (I can't find the source for now) the raster cache only improves performance on mobile in specific cases, and should not help desktop. The raster cache should be made optional.

title: Expose `onAttach` and `onDetach` in `PageController` constructor
body:
### Is there an existing issue for this?

- [X] I have searched the [existing issues](https://github.com/flutter/flutter/issues)
- [X] I have read the [guide to filing a bug](https://flutter.dev/docs/resources/bug-reports)

### Use case

The new `onAttach` and `onDetach` in `ScrollController` are currently not usable with `PageController` since its constructor doesn't include them, and extending classes can't set the fields on `ScrollController`

As discussed in #124823:

>This resolves a long held pain point for developers. When using a scroll controller, there is not scroll position until the scrollable widget is built, and almost all methods of notification are only triggered when scrolling happens. Adding these two methods will help developers gain access to the scroll position when it is created. A common workaround for this was using a post frame callback to access controller.position after the first frame, but this is ripe for issues such as having multiple positions attached to the controller, or the scrollable no longer existing after that post frame callback. I think this can also be helpful for folks to debug cases when the scroll controller has multiple positions attached.

### Proposal

Add the callbacks to the `PageController` constructors.

title: `FilterChip` should have `DeletableChipAttributes`/`trailing` to match Material 3 spec.
body:
### Is there an existing issue for this?

- [X] I have searched the [existing issues](https://github.com/flutter/flutter/issues)
- [X] I have read the [guide to filing a bug](https://flutter.dev/docs/resources/bug-reports)

### Use case

I'm trying to present several filters that a user can choose from, like shown [here](https://m3.material.io/components/chips/guidelines#11bc122b-5203-4cf5-bb12-470d86ecd3b5) in the material 3 spec. 

Additionally the user should be able to create more filter with a custom value. These custom filters should be deletable. Such a filter chip with the "X" symbol is shown in the material 3 spec [right here](https://m3.material.io/components/chips/guidelines#1ecdeead-f934-4f30-950d-25518eab90f4).   

Note: In the last link the description says: "[...]  filter chips may contain a trailing icon to directly remove the chip [...]" but subtext beneath the illustration says: "The X close icon indicates that a user can unselect the filter chip". I'm very sure "unselect" is wrong and should be replaced with "delete", since one can already unselect by tapping on the chip and as the "X" means deleting on other chip types.

### Proposal

Add `DeletableChipAttributes` to `FilterChip`.

Note: As there is also an [example with a "trailing menu item"](https://m3.material.io/components/chips/guidelines#dcf3eb06-5f81-4f7d-8299-e32a950d8572) maybe there should be a `trailing` attribute instead of only `onDeleted` etc? But as `_RawChip` specifically mentions `deleteIcon` I'm not sure if this would require a refactoring of other chip types as well.

title: `ExpansionTile`: Support Disabling User Input
body:
### Is there an existing issue for this?

- [X] I have searched the [existing issues](https://github.com/flutter/flutter/issues)
- [X] I have read the [guide to filing a bug](https://flutter.dev/docs/resources/bug-reports)

### Use case

> Please tell us the problem you are running into that led to you wanting
a new feature.

We have a vertical list of `ExpansionTile`'s, and would like the ability to disable user interaction on the `ExpansionTile`, while simultaneously allowing it's `children` to still be clickable. In our case, each `ExpansionTile` contains a grouping of cells, and we want the `ExpansionTile` that contains the currently selected item to be locked open. All of the other `ExpansionTile`'s in the list should be able to be expanded or collapsed; only the `ExpansionTile` with the currently selected item should be locked open. Currently, disabling toggling an `ExpansionTile` is not supported.

As an example, here's a screenshot of a list of `ExpansionTile`'s ("Every Element", "The Media Page", "The Question Page", "The Form Page"):
![CleanShot 2023-09-29 at 13 26 47](https://github.com/flutter/flutter/assets/6300971/396aa5d9-8e1a-4633-a73b-d4e5be50fe4f)

In this example, if you had the content selected 'Address of your favorite store', then that group (`ExpansionTile`) should be locked open, since that's what you currently have selected. The other `ExpansionTile`'s _should_ be toggle-able.

> Describe the alternative solutions you've considered. Is there a package
on pub.dev/flutter that already solves this?

1. Initially, I tried using an `AbsorbPointer` that wrapped the `ExpansionTile` to disallow it from being toggled. That worked, but also disabled clicking on the children of the `ExpansionTile`.
2. I considered using the package https://pub.dev/packages/advance_expansion_tile, but it looks outdated and unmaintained. There don't appear to be any other equivalent packages on pub.dev.
3. I considered copy-pasting the `ExpansionTile` Flutter Widget code and editing it to support this use-case, but decided against it since that would essentially 'freeze' the code in our repo and prevent us from getting updates to this Widget that Flutter releases in the future. It also prevents others from benefitting from this change.

### Proposal

The `ExpansionTile` class constructor should be updated to take another parameter that controls if user interaction is enabled or not (a `bool`, perhaps named `locked`). It should be pretty easy to save this `bool` in the `ExpansionTile` class, and then reference it to enable / disable user interaction in the `onTap` handler of the `ListTile` in the `_ExpansionTileState` class.

Note: I also considered if the `ExpansionTile`'s `trailing` property should be affected by this change, but currently think it makes the most sense to leave it as-is. One option would be to disable it if the widget is locked. Another option would be to hide it entirely if the widget is locked. This would likely make sense for the majority of the cases where the `trailing` property is a disclosure icon, but in some cases it might be a different icon (or a different type of Widget entirely). Given this, I think it makes the most sense to leave the `trailing` property unaffected by this change, as consumers of the `ExpansionTile` class can control the behavior of the `trailing` property as needed.

title: [flutter_markdown] please allow custom block elements
body:
### Is there an existing issue for this?

- [X] I have searched the [existing issues](https://github.com/flutter/flutter/issues)
- [X] I have read the [guide to filing a bug](https://flutter.dev/docs/resources/bug-reports)

### Use case

I tried to add my own custom block element using a `blockSyntaxes: const [NoteSyntax()],` declaration in the `Markdown` widget. My `NoteSyntax` creates an `Element('note', children)` which then eventually gets passed to the visitor that creates widgets.

I tried to add my own builder using `builders: {'note': NoteBuilder()},` in the `Markdown` widget, but this is never called because `MarkdownBuilder.visitElementBefore` uses a private function `_isBlockTag` to determine whether the provided `Element` is a block element by comparing its tag to a hardcoded list of tags. 

Therefore, it incorrectly thinks my `Element` is an inline element and crashes [^1] because `_addParentInlineIfNeeded` wants add an inline element that requires a `tag` but the parent of my block node is the document which has no tag.

[^1]: See line 632 of `builder.dart`, `style: styleSheet.styles[tag!],`.

### Proposal

I'd suggest to dispatch the `isBlock` call to the element itself – or let me extend that list.

title: [Impeller] Support indirect command encoding in the HAL.
ScaleUpdateDetails should give sourceTimeStamp
body:
### Is there an existing issue for this?

- [X] I have searched the [existing issues](https://github.com/flutter/flutter/issues)
- [X] I have read the [guide to filing a bug](https://flutter.dev/docs/resources/bug-reports)

### Use case

Similar to https://github.com/flutter/flutter/issues/43833. We should be able to get the `sourceTimeStamp` being that you can do so in onPanUpdate like so:
```dart
onPanUpdate: (DragUpdateDetails details){
  print(details.sourceTimeStamp);
}
``` 
My specific use case involves converting a scale event to a vertical drag event which requires `sourceTimeStamp`. A perhaps more reasonable use case would be to track the velocity of a "pan" event (meaning a pan event from a scale event) in eg. `InteractiveViewer` using a `VelocityTracker`, which requires a `time` in its `addPosition` method. This could be useful when eg. trying to detect when a user wants to close the `InteractiveViewer`.

### Proposal

I'm not sure where `sourceTimeStamp` comes from. It doesn't seem to be used in `gesture_detector.dart` so I don't know where to add it. If scale really is a superset of pan I imagine it should not be difficult to add it. If anyone knows how to add it I would like to know. I see it is defined in `drag_details.dart` for pan but I can't imagine adding `sourceTimeStamp` to `ScaleUpdateDetails` in `scale.dart` alone would be enough.

title: Support deferred components on desktop platform(MacOS/Windows/Linux)
body:
### Is there an existing issue for this?

- [X] I have searched the [existing issues](https://github.com/flutter/flutter/issues)
- [X] I have read the [guide to filing a bug](https://flutter.dev/docs/resources/bug-reports)

### Use case
1. On the desktop side, we want to achieve code dynamic based on different interfaces which rely on the implementation of deferred components, so we can only globally update app. so, we cannot only update a certain part.
2. We have two different flutter process and two `app.so` in one application. We want use deferred components to reduce app size by sharing the same compiled file(`flutter framework` compile separately)

Related:
- https://github.com/flutter/flutter/issues/79327. 

### Proposal
Deferred components was implemented for Android in https://github.com/flutter/flutter/issues/57617. We should support similar functionality on desktop(MacOS/Windows/Linux).

1. Provider APIs like `LoadDartDeferredLibrary` and `LoadDartDeferredLibraryError` and `RequestDartDeferredLibrary` on `embedder.h`
3. Support platform code in shell/platform/(darwin|windows|linux)
4. Add build tools params to build part aot files.

title: RawAutocomplete: Add a call to hide suggestion box
body:
### Is there an existing issue for this?

- [X] I have searched the [existing issues](https://github.com/flutter/flutter/issues)
- [X] I have read the [guide to filing a bug](https://flutter.dev/docs/resources/bug-reports)

### Use case

I'm creating an app with a lot of Textfields on a single page. Most of them show Autocomplete options with RawAutocomplete. When the Autocomplete drop-out is open, the user is unable to really navigate the page. The user is unable to close the Autocomplete without the textfield losing focus or selecting an option:

![image](https://github.com/flutter/flutter/assets/109872040/dd10295b-60da-4c6d-a80e-eb98eb4c593f)


### Proposal

There should be an option to hide the drop-down in-app. The options should re-appear when the user starts typing again.

Looking at the code, there are only four criterias defined: https://github.com/flutter/flutter/blob/901077bf0ed5c4280cc749c6906c85f42b356b6c/packages/flutter/lib/src/widgets/autocomplete.dart#L327-L329

1. `_userHidOptions` is true
2. Textfield is in focus
3. Nothing is selected
4. There are options available

Criteria 3 & 4 are used for the normal function of Autocomplete and not relevant here.

Criteria 2 can be used, but leads to odd behavior: The complete textfield loses focus, and the keyboard disappears.

The remaining one is `_userHidOptions`. It looks like this is doing exactly what I want: the options are hidden, but the focus remains, and the options re-appear once the user starts typing again.

It is set to true by `_hideOptions()`, which is triggered by a  `DismissIntent` (ESC key is pressed). Unfortunately, you cannot send the event properly yourself from the `fieldViewBuilder` func.

It would be great if a callback or similar can be integrated to trigger a similar to behavior like `_hideOptions()` does.

title: Platform Isolate
body:
This is a tracking bug for the Platform Isolate feature. The design details are still being worked out, but the general idea is to provide a way of using FFI to invoke native APIs that can only be be called from the platform thread.

Some related bugs: https://github.com/flutter/flutter/issues/113099, https://github.com/flutter/flutter/issues/28310, https://github.com/dart-lang/sdk/issues/52106, https://github.com/dart-lang/sdk/issues/38315, https://github.com/dart-lang/sdk/issues/42570, https://github.com/dart-lang/sdk/issues/46943, https://github.com/dart-lang/native/issues/463

Remaining work tasks after initial PR landed

- Allow calling `runInPlatformThread()` from helper isolates: https://github.com/flutter/flutter/issues/150584
- In `PlatformIsolateManager::RemovePlatformIsolate` and `PlatformIsolateManager::ShutdownPlatformIsolates`, find a way of asserting that we're on the platform thread: https://github.com/flutter/flutter/issues/150586

title: Add the ability to clip shapes other than `RoundedRectangle` in `ExpansionTile`
body:
### Is there an existing issue for this?

- [X] I have searched the [existing issues](https://github.com/flutter/flutter/issues)
- [X] I have read the [guide to filing a bug](https://flutter.dev/docs/resources/bug-reports)

### Use case

Currently, the `ExpansionTile` does not clip the `RoundedRectangleBorder` (a common use case) in either its expanded or collapsed state. This issue has been addressed in https://github.com/flutter/flutter/pull/135855.

However, implementing a similar fix for other shapes, such as the `StadiumBorder` and `BeveledRectangleBorder`, is more complex. A comprehensive solution is needed within the `ShapeBorder` implementation that can handle border radii for all shape types. This would eliminate the need to check for every shape with a border radius within the `ExpansionTile` widget. It's also worth noting that the `StadiumBorder` doesn't even provide access to its border radius.

### Proposal

Add the ability to clip shapes in `ExpansionTile` other than `RoundedRectangle` when such a method exists in `ShapeBorder`.

body:

- [X] I have read the [guide to filing a bug](https://flutter.dev/docs/resources/bug-reports)

### Use case

I am currently having difficulties to detect if a mouse right click or mouse middle click has happened outside of a widget. This is really necessary to display a custom menus in a desktop application.

Currently, TapRegion widget only detects left mouse click for onTapInside and onTapOutside. Thus, there is no way currently to detect if a right click or middle click event has happened outside of the region of a widget. On the other-hand, GestureDetector can detect right click etc but no way to detect if the click has happened outside of the GestureDetector.

### Proposal

It would be really nice to have TapRegion detect all kinds of mouse button click event.

title: [pigeon] Add a protocol for Flutter APIs in the Swift generator
body:
In https://github.com/flutter/packages/pull/5159 we needed a binary messenger mock, which makes the test fragile to future changes to that class, and also dependent on some Pigeon internals. What we really want there is a mock of the FlutterApi that we've defined within the plugin, which would be more robust and easier to use. However, in Swift we can't mock without having a protocol.

For Flutter APIs, instead of generating just the class, we should generate a protocol with the methods defined in the Pigeon definition, and then the current class as implementing that protocol. Then unit tests can use an exposed-for-test initializer that passes in a mock API implementation (with the real init calling that helper with the current `IosQuickActionsFlutterApi(binaryMessenger: messenger)`), and there won't need to be a mock binary messenger at all.

title: `flutter run --local-engine` should generate overridden dependencies for `sky_engine` automatically.
body:
Right now, when I modify Dart code in my local engine build and want to test those with flutter code from the flutter repo, I run something like this:

```
flutter run -d linux --local-engine-host=host_debug_unopt --local-engine=host_debug_unopt
```

The problem is that this fails to compile because the local version of sky_engine doesn't match the Dart changes I've made in the engine, and I need to add something like this to the `pubspec.yaml` of the thing I am running:

```yaml
dependency_overrides:
  sky_engine:
    path: /usr/local/home/gspencer/code/engine/src/out/host_debug_unopt/gen/dart-pkg/sky_engine
```

Making these changes now works for running the app I want to run, but the IDE still shows errors until I also add that same stanza to the `packages/flutter/pubspec.yaml` file.

Which means I need to update the override if I switch to another build type, and I need to remember to revert the pubspecs before committing.

It wouldn't fix the IDE problem, but when I specify `--local-engine` to `flutter run`, it would make sense to also transparently add the correct override to the pubspec for running the application.  It might mean that it somehow needs to supply a different pubspec than the one in the app's directory because ideally I wouldn't have to revert the file before committing.

Perhaps what needs to exist is an environment variable that both the IDE and the flutter tool, and probably dart too, listen to that would point to a file with any global dependency overrides in it. Then that file could be written by `flutter run --local-engine` as a temporary file, and it would set the environment variable. The IDE would need to have an additional setting for which engine to use so it knows what to set the environment variable to.

This would allow setting the local engine to transparently use the correct Dart files for sky_engine.

cc @DanTup

title: Clean up memory leaks in Flutter Framework, phase 2.
body:
Looking for **volunteers** to help with this!!! 🚀

Issues: [all](https://github.com/issues?q=is%3Aissue+label%3A%22a%3A+leak+tracking%22+), [open](https://github.com/issues?q=is%3Aopen+is%3Aissue+label%3A%22a%3A+leak+tracking%22).
PRs: [all](https://github.com/pulls?q=is%3Apr+label%3A%22a%3A+leak+tracking%22+), [open](https://github.com/pulls?q=is%3Aopen+is%3Apr+archived%3Afalse+label%3A%22a%3A+leak+tracking%22+).


Focus areas: 
@polina-c: kick off cleanup for notGCed and [integration with testWidgets](https://docs.google.com/document/d/1bXannXQ8rEtfwxEVd7LDo7xiVu5-QxRoall28MIIFss/edit#heading=h.dk8qogiupft4)


Bigger issue: https://github.com/flutter/flutter/issues/134787

# Goal of this issue 
1. All disposables in FlutterFramework (listed below) should be instrumented for leak tracking ([example](https://github.com/flutter/flutter/pull/137309)).
2. There should not be `.withIgnored(` under [flutter/test](https://github.com/flutter/flutter/tree/master/packages/flutter/test) (or `.withIgnored(` should have a comment that explains why the ignored leaks are ok).

# How to turn on leak tracking?

Leak tracking is now off by default for Flutter Framework. 
To turn it on, follow [the instruction](https://github.com/flutter/flutter/blob/d8a5f3d11bd4216c63766febe0cfb15bf79f44af/packages/flutter/test/flutter_test_config.dart#L30).
To check your changes for leaks, temporarily turn on leak tracking, then, to merge your change, turn it off.

This PR is regularly refreshed and runs bots with leak tracking on, to verify current status of flutter master: https://github.com/flutter/flutter/pull/138581.

# Steps

The goal should be achieved by iteratively instrumenting the objects and removing them from ignore lists:

1. Identify area that you plan to work on and comment on this issue so that I add you to the focus areas above so that we do not duplicate work. If your plans changed, also let me know.  

2. Instrument a new class:
    a. Create PR with instrumentation for one more object, mark it as draft, and check if bots pass
    a. If all bots pass, merge PR
    b. If number of failures is small and they are easy to fix, fix them in the same PR
    c. If failures are massive or unclear, add `withIgnored(` for the instrumented class either [in root folder](https://github.com/flutter/flutter/blob/fe96c2b8dfcbdf45a8e493bca3192d75f7e98ccf/packages/flutter/test/flutter_test_config.dart#L37), or in sub-folder, or in a test file (in setUpAll), or for individual tests.

3. Remove ignored leaks
    a. Identify `withIgnored` on any level ([flutter_test_config.dart](https://github.com/flutter/flutter/blob/961f955af2b139e31117d963e8c533912e2cc0b9/packages/flutter/test/flutter_test_config.dart#L33C18-L33C18), test library or individual test), create draft PR with removed ignored leak types or classes and check if bots pass
    b. If bots pass remove all inclusions (withTracked) for this class/leak-type in subsets of tests and merge the PR
    c. If bots fail, return the `withIgnored`, and add `withTracked` for the leak to one of the failed tests
    d. Check the list of issues/PRs for work in progress for the failure.
    e. If you know how to fix the leak, fix it and merge the PR ([example](https://github.com/flutter/flutter/pull/137812))
    f. If the fix is unclear, remove`withTracked`, create an [issue](https://github.com/flutter/flutter/issues/138455), link the issue in TODO for the test, and merge the PR

4. While testWidgets is not integrated with leak tracking, convert `testWidgets` to `testWidgetsWithLeakTracking` as instructed [in phase 1](https://github.com/dart-lang/leak_tracker/issues/134)

# Templates

Template for steps to repro for leak issues:
1. Locate the test: \<perm link to the test\>
2. (Either) Add parameter `leakTesting = LeakTesting.settings.withTracked(classes: ['TheClass'])`
3. (Or) Remove parameter  `leakTesting = LeakTesting.settings.withIgnored(...)`
5. See the test failing

Template for TODO:
```
TODO(<your alias>): fix the leak and remove ignore (or add tracking for <name of class>)
<link to the issue>
```
Note: your alias in TODO does not mean your obligation to fix the issue, it just indicates you have some knowledge about it.

# Classes to instrument 
Instrumentation for test-only classes is not a goal, but if it is needed to catch other leaks, feel free to instrument them.


- [x] _RouteEntry
- [x] AnimationController
- [ ] AnimationEagerListenerMixin
- [x] BannerPainter
- [x] ChangeNotifier
- [ ] CurvedAnimation
- [x] DisposableBuildContext
- [x] Element
- [x] GestureRecognizer
- [x] Image
- [ ] ImageInfo
- [x] ImageStreamCompleterHandle
- [x] Layer
- [x] MultiDragPointerState
- [x] OverlayEntry
- [x] PerformanceModeRequestHandle
- [x] Picture
- [x] PipelineOwner
- [x] RenderObject
- [x] RestorationBucket
- [x] Route
- [ ] ScrollDragController
- [ ] SelectionOverlay
- [x] SemanticOwner
- [ ] SnapshotPainter
- [x] State
- [x] TextPainter
- [ ] TextSelectionOverlay


(Let me know if I missed something)

title: [shared_preferences] Support namespaces/prefixes for multi-account apps
body:
### Is there an existing issue for this?

- [X] I have searched the [existing issues](https://github.com/flutter/flutter/issues)
- [X] I have read the [guide to filing a bug](https://flutter.dev/docs/resources/bug-reports)

### Use case

I have an app that supports multiple accounts and want separate `SharedPreferences` per account.

### Proposal

If an app supports multiple accounts and wants to use separate preferences per account the current `shared_preferences` implementation does not suffice.

I suggest allowing `SharedPreferences.setPrefix()` to be allowed to be called multiple times (currently it throws an error) and to invalidate the cache each time `setPrefix()` is called.

This would allow multi-account apps to separate preferences per account.

title: Add support for fixed accent colors
body:
### Is there an existing issue for this?

- [X] I have searched the [existing issues](https://github.com/flutter/flutter/issues)
- [X] I have read the [guide to filing a bug](https://flutter.dev/docs/resources/bug-reports)

### Use case

[Part of #91605 ]
Material 3 has introduced a set of new color roles that retain the same color in the light and dark themes collectively referred to as fixed accent colors See: https://m3.material.io/styles/color/the-color-system/color-roles#232e7275-6ef2-4b93-afa6-c5f76e378ced

New surface color roles have also been added: (See #137679)
These changes appear to be part of a wider set of updates to the color system, see [this](https://youtu.be/vnDhq8W98O4?t=649). 


### Color Roles

#### Fixed Accent Colors
See: https://m3.material.io/styles/color/the-color-system/color-roles#232e7275-6ef2-4b93-afa6-c5f76e378ced
| Color Role         | Tone: Light | Tone: Dark |
| ------------------ | ----------- | ---------- |
| Primary Fixed      | P-90        | P-90       |
| Primary Fixed Dim  | P-80        | P-80       |
| Secondary Fixed    | S-90        | S-90       |
| Secondary Fixed Dim| S-80        | S-80       |
| Tertiary Fixed     | T-90        | T-90       |
| Tertiary Fixed Dim | T-80        | T-80       |

#### On Fixed Accent Colors
See: https://m3.material.io/styles/color/the-color-system/color-roles#6a242aff-e606-43d5-a7de-988facc90968
| Color Role                 | Tone: Light | Tone: Dark |
| -------------------------- | ----------- | ---------- |
| On Primary Fixed           | P-10        | P-10       |
| On Primary Fixed Variant   | P-30        | P-30       |
| On Secondary Fixed         | S-10        | S-10       |
| On Secondary Fixed Variant | S-30        | S-30       |
| On Tertiary Fixed          | T-10        | T-10       |
| On Tertiary Fixed Variant  | T-30        | T-30       |


### Proposal

Update `ColorScheme` to include the above roles. The current `ColorScheme.fromSeed` implementation uses the `Scheme` class underneath which is planned to be deprecated. For more info, see #137679.

body:
### Is there an existing issue for this?

### Steps to reproduce

1. Run the attached demo app
2. Horizontally scroll the text in the dropdown menu

### Expected results

I would like to have an option which disables horizontal scrolling of the text.
When i limit the width of the dropdown menu i am aware that menu item's labels can have a larger text and therefore are not fully visible.
At the moment it seems that the text is always scrollable when i drag it horizontally.
Since i have to horizontally scroll some DropdownMenu instances in a Row the scrolling of an overflowing text inside a menu at the same time is quite disturbing.

title: Add an event to support post-submit actions when all post-submit tests finish
body:
It is not uncommon that we need to do post-submit actions when all tests finish. For example:
1) Packages repo needs to [waits for all post-submit tests to pass](https://github.com/flutter/plugins/blob/main/.github/workflows/release.yml#L37-L47) before publishing @stuartmorgan 
2) Engine repo needs to roll latest version to framework when all post-submit tests pass (WIP) @XilaiZhang 

We need to have a generic way to flag when all post-submit tests pass. Eg. this event can then be consumed from GitHub actions to act accordingly.

/cc @godofredoc

title: No offline check when performing various Flutter CLI steps
body:
### Is there an existing issue for this?

- [X] I have searched the [existing issues](https://github.com/flutter/flutter/issues)
- [X] I have read the [guide to filing a bug](https://flutter.dev/docs/resources/bug-reports)

### Use case

Our attempts to onboard Flutter into a major bank conflicts with our regulatory control procedures. We cannot use any applications which will go out to the public Internet - our networks do not allow this. 

Installing Flutter via git repository is a non-starter.

We begin with the Flutter SDK packages [available here](https://docs.flutter.dev/release/archive), and set the developers environment up thus

* PUB_HOSTED_URL set to our internal Pub server
* FLUTTER_STORAGE_BASE_URL set to our internal Google mirror
* Remove the Git origin remote
* Disable telemetry
* Disable analytics

As we do this, the calls to disable telemetry and disable analytics have long network timeouts associated with them before the command concludes.

The runner does not accept --offline or --no-version-check

```shell
/bin/flutter -vv --no-version-check --disable-telemetry
[   +5 ms] executing: sw_vers -productName
[  +30 ms] Exit code 0 from: sw_vers -productName
[        ] macOS
[        ] executing: sw_vers -productVersion
[  +20 ms] Exit code 0 from: sw_vers -productVersion
[        ] 13.6.1
[        ] executing: sw_vers -buildVersion
[  +25 ms] Exit code 0 from: sw_vers -buildVersion
[        ] 22G313
[        ] executing: uname -m
[  +15 ms] Exit code 0 from: uname -m
[        ] arm64
[  +18 ms] executing: sysctl hw.optional.arm64
[  +21 ms] Exit code 0 from: sysctl hw.optional.arm64
[        ] hw.optional.arm64: 1
[  +37 ms] Analytics reporting disabled.
[+75107 ms] Running 0 shutdown hooks
[        ] Shutdown hooks complete
[        ] exiting with code 0
```

### Proposal

> This is an attempt to bring another use case to #129143. That ticket was closed after a discussion, but there isn't a summary of the discussion and the reasoning for the "won't fix" resolution. Can a summary behind the decision be posted on that ticket?

Proposing a global configuration (e.g. `--hermetic`) that we can use to truly prevent Flutter from making network calls. 

Ideally, when `--hermetic` is configured, the following actions take place

* Running `flutter upgrade` or `flutter downgrade` an attempt to upgrade/downgrade will result in a message indicating that external network requests have been disabled (Basically, any Git-related action - including channel switch - supports this message)
* New Flutter version checks do not occur
* Apparently, the delay I am seeing in disabling analytics is caused by this? "If you opt out of analytics, an opt-out event will be sent, and then no further information will be sent by the Flutter tool." - I would obviously not want that to happen if I have globally configured a `--hermetic` flag
* Perhaps the message "Flutter assets will be downloaded from $FLUTTER_STORAGE_BASE_URL. Make sure you trust this source!" could be disabled when the `--hermetic` flag is set. Or maybe, it could be mentioned on the initial Flutter command, and then no longer. It frightens the developers. :)

title: Corporate users need a way to populate an internal mirror of FLUTTER_STORAGE_BASE_URL
body:
### Is there an existing issue for this?

- [X] I have searched the [existing issues](https://github.com/flutter/flutter/issues)
- [X] I have read the [guide to filing a bug](https://flutter.dev/docs/resources/bug-reports)

### Steps to reproduce

Think of some large, highly regulated companies in the US as operating in a restricted Internet capacity (even more restrictive than China).

Our developer laptops do not have free access to the Internet, nor are we allowed to poke holes in our firewall for an exception to Google servers.

When Flutter is executed, depending on the command and the OS and chipset, Flutter will go out to a network location (base url specified by `FLUTTER_STORAGE_BASE_URL`) to download any number of support files and binaries specific to that version of Flutter. 

Within a firewall (corporate or China), we have the ability to change the base URL specified by FLUTTER_STORAGE_BASE_URL to a reachable URL for the current network. Any calls made by Flutter (and a number of other tools), will respect the environment variable to locate files.

HOWEVER, without the ability to mirror Google's default base URL for supplementary Flutter content, we haven't the means to supply the necessary files to our internal "mirror" to be downloaded.

The default base url for that environment variable is `https://storage.googleapis.com`, however the folder structure is indeterminate. There is no way for someone outside of Google to recreate the contents of the `flutter_infra_release` folder and its subfolders without a manifest of all files a particular Flutter version requires.

There is a China mirror, because Google copies files to that location. There is no ability for a corporation to do so.


### Actual results

Any downloads of external content attempted by Flutter results in a network timeout or unreachable error.

```shell
flutter -vv --disable-telemetry
Downloading Darwin arm64 Dart SDK from Flutter engine a794cf2681c6c9fe7b260e0e84de96298dc9c18b...
  % Total    % Received % Xferd  Average Speed   Time    Time     Time  Current
                                 Dload  Upload   Total   Spent    Left  Speed
100    19  100    19    0     0     90      0 --:--:-- --:--:-- --:--:--    94
[/usr/local/jpmc/flutter-3.13.6/bin/cache/dart-sdk-darwin-arm64.zip]
  End-of-central-directory signature not found.  Either this file is not
  a zipfile, or it constitutes one disk of a multi-part archive.  In the
  latter case the central directory and zipfile comment will be found on
  the last disk(s) of this archive.
unzip:  cannot find zipfile directory in one of /usr/local/jpmc/flutter-3.13.6/bin/cache/dart-sdk-darwin-arm64.zip or
        /usr/local/jpmc/flutter-3.13.6/bin/cache/dart-sdk-darwin-arm64.zip.zip, and cannot find /usr/local/jpmc/flutter-3.13.6/bin/cache/dart-sdk-darwin-arm64.zip.ZIP, period.

It appears that the downloaded file is corrupt; please try again.
If this problem persists, please report the problem at:
  https://github.com/flutter/flutter/issues/new?template=1_activation.yml
```

How might we be able to create our own mirror of Flutter's on-demand files?

### Logs

N/A

### Flutter Doctor output

Cannot run `flutter doctor`

title: Allow (on demand/toggle) running post-submits as pre-submits
body:
### Is there an existing issue for this?

- [X] I have searched the [existing infra issues](https://github.com/flutter/flutter/issues?q=is%3Aopen+is%3Aissue+label%3Ateam-infra)

### Type of Request

feature request

### Infrastructure Environment

I'd like to see this implemented in the code that parses and/or understands `.ci.yaml`, which, IIRC, is Cocoon?

---

Similar requests:

- @gaaclarke's https://github.com/flutter/flutter/issues/111418.

### What is happening?

## Background

The engine repo has insufficient capacity to run every kind of test on every kind of platform on every commit.

As a result, we run many tests as [`presubmit: false`](https://github.com/flutter/engine/blob/main/.ci.yaml), i.e.: [this config](https://github.com/flutter/engine/blob/eba757803a6f3144d4875c54db51a0add551c72c/.ci.yaml#L245-L248). That works fine for the vast majority of commits, but sometimes we are changing things that are _only_ exhaustively tested by postsubmit.

Two recent examples:

1. https://github.com/flutter/flutter/issues/138381; 
  due to not running the clang-tidy postsubmits.

2. https://github.com/flutter/engine/pull/47397;
  caused a device lab failure due to not running the device lab physical devices.

## Proposal

The currently suggested workflow is to make _manual edits_ to `.ci.yaml`, and remember to revert them before merging.

While we could bike shed on granularity (like a `test: physical-android-devices`), I'd like to propose starting with a simple Github label (i.e. `test: include-postsubmit`, or whatever follows our label philosophy) that treats `presubmit: false` as `presubmit: true`.

That should be sufficient to see, particularly on the engine repo, if we can catch more of these issues in pre-submit.

### Steps to reproduce

_No response_

### Expected results

_No response_

title: [cocoon] Throttle swarming presubmit tasks during peak hours of users consuming a large amount of infra quota
body:
Occasionally, someone will send a large number of cleanup PRs which consumes a large chunk of presubmit capacity. To protect overall SLOs for the community, we should deprioritize this user for the day.

Peak hours would be around 10 AM - 3 PM PST on US workdays.

This could be some metric based on the number of builds being scheduled. For example, we could batch it in groups of 50 targets. These numbers can be tweaked to better match our infra capacity:

0 - 150: No impact
150 - 250: Slight reduction
250+: Lowest priority

Cocoon can leave a comment on the PR as an FYI that the user is being throttled for using too much capacity.

title: Provide ability to override `NavigationBar` indicator ink response overlay
Build hook for Flutter Web?
body:
### Is there an existing issue for this?

- [X] I have searched the [existing issues](https://github.com/flutter/flutter/issues)
- [X] I have read the [guide to filing a bug](https://flutter.dev/docs/resources/bug-reports)

### Use case

Hi thanks for the great framework! When developing https://github.com/fzyzcjy/flutter_rust_bridge v2, we are having the following scenario: We need to compile some Rust code into WASM, which is used by Flutter code.

For the native platforms, the build can be invoked easily. For example, in Android, we can just modify the build.gradle and let it invoke the build commands; similar for iOS, windows, macos, linux. However, IMHO there is no place to inject such "hooks" when building.

Surely, we can just create another tool that wraps the "build rust + call flutter run" process (that's what we are doing currently) - but that looks suboptimal.

I guess it is somehow related to the native assets https://github.com/dart-lang/sdk/issues/50565 / https://github.com/flutter/flutter/issues/129757, but this is just a (small) part of what native asset system is going to cover, and seems to be also possibly orthogonal (e.g. today's Android/iOS/... build hook can be implemented, but without native assets), so I make a separate issue here.

### Proposal

(see above)

title: Support `DismissDirection` in `SnackBarThemeData`
body:
### Is there an existing issue for this?

- [X] I have searched the [existing issues](https://github.com/flutter/flutter/issues)
- [X] I have read the [guide to filing a bug](https://flutter.dev/docs/resources/bug-reports)

### Use case

Currently, `SnackBar` supports `DismissDirection` as a widget parameter. However it is not supported in `SnackBarThemeData` and cannot be added to the overall app theme. For comparison, `SnackBarBehavior` can be passed in both `SnackBar` and `SnackBarThemeData`.

This means whenever I create a SnackBar, I need to explicitely specify the `DismissDirection` in every SnackBar instantiation when it would be easier to just define in my app theme. 

### Proposal

Add a parameter for specifying `DismissDirection` in `SnackBarThemeData`.

```
SnackBarThemeData(
    dismissDirection: DismissDirection.horizontal,
)
```

title: [go-router] Add a ShellRoute.indexed constructor.
body:
### Is there an existing issue for this?

- [X] I have searched the [existing issues](https://github.com/flutter/flutter/issues)
- [X] I have read the [guide to filing a bug](https://flutter.dev/docs/resources/bug-reports)

### Use case

Go router currently provides the StatefulShellRoute.indexedStack() constructor. It works well when you are working with any index-based navbar and want to keep the widget state. But there is no clean way to dispose or reload the state once you are going to another location / coming back to the old location. The lack of methods to dispose the state is a problem with big apps especially.

In order to achieve a clean dispose-architecture, you can use the normal ShellRoute, but when working with a NavigationRail e.g. you have much boilerplate code to manage for initial selection, pushing routes if the selection changes and so on. 

### Proposal

A constructor like "ShellRoute.indexed()" would be great for this use case, with getters like in "StatefulShellRoute.indexedStack()" (getter for index and the ability to go to a route with an index).

If I'm overlooking another way of achieving this in a clean manner, please let me know. 

If needed, I can provide some minimal code samples of my current implementation and what I'm looking for with "ShellRoute.indexed", although I think it should be pretty clear.

title: Improve and fix MenuAnchor/MenuItemButton UX for desktop
body:
MenuAnchor for Material 3 is great for contextual menus but there are some minor issues related to accessibility. Specifically, keyboard navigation does not auto-select an element when opening the menu. This causes some UX confusion where the user has to navigate further to access the menu he or she just opened, which in my case is not user friendly.

Setup:
- Material 3
- Windows 10 22H2
- Flutter 3.16.2

Relevant PR:
- [ ] https://github.com/flutter/flutter/pull/139396 MenuItemButton doesn't have an `autofocus` attribute, thereby making it harder to navigate with the keyboard. 


Thanks Flutter Team!

title: Allow arbitrary foreground and background elements for `ButtonStyle`
body: ### Is there an existing issue for this? - [X] I have searched the [existing issues](https://github.com/flutter/flutter/issues) - [X] I have read the [guide to filing a bug](https://flutter.dev/docs/resources/bug-reports) ### Use case I want to add fairly arbitrary visual details to my app's `ElevatedButton`'s (this feature request applies to all the material button types, but I'll use `ElevatedButton` as a stand in). Here are some examples: 1. Adding a linear/radial/etc gradient 2. Adding a border 3. Using a semi-opaque image to texture the button 4. Using an arbitrary box shadow instead of the material elevation system 5. Placing .svgs above, behind and around the button as visual accents As-is, `ButtonStyle` limits me to a very narrow range of style customization: shape, color, elevation... I could create my own button system, but the material buttons do an enormous amount of extremely helpful things (manage splash factory, handle material state, tune visual density to match the platform, etc) that would be a huge hassle to reverse engineer. Here are some examples of random apps in the wild that have buttons that are not achievable without heavy workarounds. My app. I use a linear gradient and a border using a complicated work around that puts every elevated button in a stack: Marvel snap. Marvel snap uses a particularly complex system containing tons of elements: 1. asymmetric shape 2. Texture 3. Texture fades to solid background in center 4. Stroke text 5. Metallic border 6. Light flare 7. And more... ### Proposal I think there are a lot of different ways to add greater customization to `ButtonStyle`. I will list a few I've thought of here, I'm very open to more suggestions! 1. Mimic `Container`'s foreground and background `BoxDecoration` arguments I don't love this solution as it's fairly limiting. For example, you can't use a `Stack` to stack elements on top of each other in this context. 2. Include foreground and background builder arguments. The builders would take in a context, the current material state(s) and the `EdgeInsets` by which the button is padded with (as they should be able to draw in and outside of the button without having to reverse engineer the complicated padding logic). I like this much more, as it gives me completely arbitrary design control. For example, even Marvel Snap's insanely complicated button would probably be doable with this system. The implementation would need to ensure the foreground child doesn't interfere with hit testing. The disadvantage is that even pretty simple customizations (like adding a linear gradient) would be pretty complicated. In both cases, the background argument would be placed between the button's child and the background `Material`. And/or the `Material` could be left out entirely if the background argument is non null. Maybe to ensure that simple customization is easy while complex customization is still possible, BOTH `BoxDecoration` and builder based customization could be provided? What this might look like to use (coded off the top of my head, probably has minor syntax errors): ```dart ElevatedButton( style: ButtonStyle( backgroundBuilder: (context, padding, materialStates, style) { final baseColor = style.resolveFor(materialStates); final shape = style.shape; return Padding( // Pad the background by the inherited padding. // Elements could be placed above this padding to draw outside // of the elevated button's background. padding: padding, child: _ClipToShape( shape: shape, child: Stack( children: [ _MyGradient(color: baseColor), _MySvgAccent(), // And so on. ], ) ), ); }, ), ); ```

[macOS] Add mouse cursor countingUpHand, countingDownHand, and countingUpAnDownHand
body:
All system mouse cursors from macOS's `CoreCursor` are planned to be included except for the following three:

* kCountingUpHandCursor
* kCountingDownHandCursor
* kCountingUpAndDownHandCursor

These cursors look like a hand counting with fingers, animating from 1 to 5 or backwards. 

There are two reason they are not included:
* During testing I found that they do not animate unless the mouse is moving. (I might be wrong, since my testing app is throwing errors.)
* They're exclusively present on macOS and I'm not aware of any uses.

If you find this cursor necessary, comment below with a use case (or better, a name) and we can discuss adding this cursor.

Related:
* https://github.com/flutter/flutter/issues/61397

title: Deeplinking web validator (Android)
body:
A deep link is a special type of URL that takes users directly to a specific page or section within an app. It contains a scheme, host, and path to open the app and navigate to the desired location. 

Deep links allow end-users to easily navigate to the relevant content in the mobile app from a mobile website. Because mobile apps generally provide better user experience, users who’re directed to an app are twice more likely to convert (e.g. make a purchase) than users who are directed to the mobile web. 

However, deeplinking setup is complicated and often error-prone. We hope to improve this deeplinking implementation experience by first providing a validator to help developers determine if their implementation is accurate. 

In Flutter applications, a deeplink setup consists of app setup and web setup. Assuming a developer starts with a fresh Flutter application, the app setup includes the adding certain settings or tags in native Android/iOS files and the dart code to handle incoming urls. The web setup involves maintaining a website that hosts a file to associate its web domain to a flutter app.

This particular issue tracks the web setup validation for Android app developed using Flutter. We will also have separate issues tracking web validation for iOS app using Flutter, and later app set up validation for both iOS and Android app using  Flutter.

title: TapRegion has no clear way to differentiate between different gestures
body:
### Use case

By default, the on screen keyboard is dismissed by non-mobile touch events, such as a click.
It is quite common to want similar behavior on mobile. That can be done by calling `unfocus` on the text fields `FocusNode` inside the `onTapCallback`. That does however mean that both taps and scrolls are handled the same, since `TapRegion` uses `PointerDownEvent` as a trigger for `onTapOutside`. (Contradictory to what the name might suggest. Compare with `onTap` and `onTapDown` in `GestureDetector`)

Having the ability to differentiate between scroll and tap would give developers the ability to close the text field on a tap, but keep it open when scrolling.

Attached below is a video of the Google Home app on Android, showing the behavior that I'm looking for.

https://github.com/flutter/flutter/assets/47989573/77d26c16-5359-44ae-8002-8e30ff88f8e8

### Proposal

`TapRegion`, should have more parameters to differentiate between different gestures, similar to `GestureDetector`. This would be a breaking change, but `onTapOutside` should behave like `onTap` of `GestureDetector`, and a new parameter with the name `onTapDownOutside` should be added that has the current behaviour. Other gestures should be handled by other parameters.

title: Rename MemoryAllocations to FlutterMemoryAllocations.
body:
**Why?**

Disposables in Dart cannot use MemoryAllocations in Flutter. So, to be leak trackable they need a dart-only class.
See detains in [the document](https://docs.google.com/document/d/1ireWmX4J5Jc6oDO1PmAXPlLdiAlCHvTz8SVC_Sg9KM8/edit?resourcekey=0-yODb_SAStMgMefYpQeX8uQ#bookmark=id.e3vlg7f7t1l).

**Why now?**

1. We want early adopters of leak tracker (for example, package owners) to use the class with the right name, so that they do not deal with renaming later.
2. It will take time for the renaming to land to flutter stable.

**Steps:**

- [x] Add FlutterMemoryAllocations and deprecate MemoryAllocations in Flutter
- [x] Document breaking change in Flutter: https://github.com/flutter/website/pull/10021
- [x] Update leak tracker to use FlutterMemoryAllocations
- [x] Roll leak tracker to G3
- [x] Upgrade flutter to new leak tracker
- [ ] Remove MemoryAllocations from Flutter

title: Release branch presubmits pass `FLUTTER_PREBUILT...=` which conflicts with Content Hashing
body:
With content-hashing, a given git hash is _no longer_ guaranteed to provide an engine build.

For example, [Linux flutter_release_builder / 1007](https://ci.chromium.org/ui/p/dart-internal/builders/flutter/Linux%20flutter_release_builder/1007/overview) correctly skipped building an engine as unnecessary, as a previous builder built the same (content-hashed) engine. However, this is in conflict with [how we provide `FLUTTER_PREBUILT_ENGINE_VERSION=`, at least in presubmits](https://github.com/flutter/cocoon/blob/b88be3ecf4e49a5a8138940cc2e91e72ba6ca84e/app_dart/lib/src/service/luci_build_service.dart#L286-L293).

From talking to @jtmcdole, we should just be able to stop providing this, at least ... for release builders? Need to double check.

title: [iOS] Flakey race condition between SetSemanticsTreeEnabled and UpdateSemantics
body:
### Steps to reproduce

1. `flutter create my_app`
2. `cd my_app`
3. `et run --config ios_debug_sim_unopt_arm64`
4. Quit the app once it launches
5. Open `my_app/ios/Runner.xcworkspace`
6. In Xcode do Product > Run 

Note: Error does not always happen since it's a race condition, you may need to do step 6 multiple times. It is more likely to happen if you [setup UIScene in your Info.plist](https://docs.flutter.dev/release/breaking-changes/uiscenedelegate#info-plist-migration), but I have seen it happen for both. Also, the crash can also happen when running from the Flutter CLI, but running from Xcode is faster and allows you to see the error log.

### Expected results

App should run fine when using a local engine

### Actual results

App crashes

```
[FATAL:flutter/shell/platform/darwin/ios/platform_view_ios.mm(156)] Check failed: accessibility_bridge_.
```

https://github.com/flutter/flutter/blob/7a13e1be3a98704f91acf2807aede12277771bf6/engine/src/flutter/shell/platform/darwin/ios/platform_view_ios.mm#L154

I tried tracing it down and it appears that occasionally `FlutterEngine` enables semantics before the `_handleFrameworkSemanticsEnabledChanged` listener is set up.

https://github.com/flutter/flutter/blob/7a13e1be3a98704f91acf2807aede12277771bf6/engine/src/flutter/shell/platform/darwin/ios/framework/Source/FlutterEngine.mm#L345-L351

https://github.com/flutter/flutter/blob/7a13e1be3a98704f91acf2807aede12277771bf6/packages/flutter/lib/src/semantics/binding.dart#L42

And therefore, the semantics tree is never enabled:
https://github.com/flutter/flutter/blob/7a13e1be3a98704f91acf2807aede12277771bf6/packages/flutter/lib/src/semantics/binding.dart#L168-L170


Likely caused by https://github.com/flutter/flutter/pull/174163.

Use default `flutter create` sample app

title: Add documentation to `DropdownMenu.onSelected` and add appropriate type constraints
body:
https://main-api.flutter.dev/flutter/material/DropdownMenu/onSelected.html

> The callback is called when a selection is made.
>
> Defaults to null. If null, only the text field is updated.

Upon reading the field / class docs, it does not tell me what `null` represents in `T?` and when I would get `null` in the callback.

Also since `null` seems to have a special meaning to the `DropdownMenu` implementation, the class needs an additional type constraint: `T extends Object` so the user can't assign a different meaning to `null`.

title: [flutter/packages] clang-format is formatting c++ headers as objc
body:
When working on packages in flutter/packages, the `format` tool incorrectly applies Objective-C formatting rules to C++ `.h` files

### Root Cause
File extension ambiguity: Both C++ and Objective-C use `.h` files

### Current Configuration

.clang-format file:
  ```yaml
  BasedOnStyle: Google
  ---
  Language: Cpp
  DerivePointerAlignment: false
  PointerAlignment: Left
  ---
  Language: ObjC
  DerivePointerAlignment: false
  PointerAlignment: Right
```

### Expected vs Actual Behavior

- Expected: C++ `.h` files formatted with PointerAlignment: Left (e.g., ```int* ptr```)
- Actual: C++ `.h` files formatted with PointerAlignment: Right (e.g., ```int *ptr```)

### To Reproduce

```sh
dart run script/tool/bin/flutter_plugin_tools.dart format --packages file_selector
```

### Possible Solution

A possible solution could be add a package-specific `.clang-format` files with C++ configuration only in packages containing C++ code

title: Use TextPainter built-in API for ellipsis in Slider
body:
### Use case

For the context, check https://github.com/flutter/flutter/pull/173324#issuecomment-3269160721.

I'm currently appending ellipses to multiline text in Slider label. Meanwhile, TextPaint supports ellipsis in built-in API:

https://github.com/flutter/flutter/blob/844edbcd91ba0aa7b1b2d8d809689c69ebeb7222/packages/flutter/lib/src/painting/text_painter.dart#L608

However, when maxLines is set, it's not working. This is blocked by https://github.com/flutter/flutter/issues/50168.  Once https://github.com/flutter/flutter/issues/50168 is fixed, we should use built-in API instead of appending ellipses manually.

### Proposal

Use TextPainter built-in API for ellipsis in Slider. https://github.com/flutter/flutter/pull/173324 is followed by this.

[Google3 Bug]: ImageStream & ImageProvider provide no way to pause GIF animations
body:
Related: https://github.com/flutter/flutter/issues/130976

### Help us understand the severity of this issue

- [ ] causing severe production issues e.g. malfunctions or data loss
- [ ] blocking next binary release
- [x] blocking a client feature launch within a quarter
- [ ] nice-to-have but does not block a launch within the next quarter

### Steps to reproduce

1. Load a GIF asset into `ImageProvider`/`ImageStream` via `CachedNetworkImage`.

### Expected results

Either one of the following:

1. The `ImageProvider`/`ImageStream` library provides a way to disable any animation of the provided image.
2. `MediaQuery.of(context).disableAnimations` is respected by the `ImageProvider`/`ImageStream` library by default.

### Actual results

1.  Neither `ImageProvider`/`ImageStream` provide a way to disable animation of an image
2. `MediaQuery.of(context).disableAnimations` is not respected by the `ImageProvider`/`ImageStream` library.

### Code sample

<details open><summary>Code sample</summary>

yaml:
```xml
dependencies:
  flutter:
    sdk: flutter
  cached_network_image: ^3.3.1 # Use the latest version
```

import 'package:cached_network_image/cached_network_image.dart';
import 'package:flutter/material.dart';

void main() => runApp(const MyApp());

class MyApp extends StatelessWidget {
  const MyApp({super.key});

  @override
  Widget build(BuildContext context) {
    return MaterialApp(
      title: 'GIF Loading with CachedNetworkImage',

        appBar: AppBar(title: const Text('Cached GIF')),
        body: Center(

  }
}
```

title: DropDownMenu secondary trailing widget (e.g. for clear action)
body: ### Use case If having a [DropDownMenu](https://api.flutter.dev/flutter/material/DropdownMenu-class.html) it is currently only possible to replace the `trailingIcon`, which is tied to an action. Internally a TextField with an IconButton is used to provide this dropdown action. For convenience it should also be possible to define more trailing actions apart from the dropdown functionality. In comparison to a TextField, where this trailing icon is usually used as a `clear` action, it should also be possible for a `DropDownMenu` to define such an action via e.g. `secondaryTrailing`, which also could hold a list to define multiple trailing widgets, in front of the current dropdown tralingIcon. ### Proposal How it currently is: Proposal: E.g. by providing a property called `secodaryTrailing` As reference: This is how its shown for a textfield with a clear icon: https://m3.material.io/components/text-fields/overview ### Workarounds For me no workarounds are known. If replacing the `trailingIcon` with a `Row` of `IconButton`s the Row would be still wrapped by the IconButton of the DropDownMenu Widget, which would result in a double wrapped IconButton, if providing a second one next to it. ### Alternative solutions We could also have a `trailing` parameter, which overrides all actions, but then it would be advisable to instead have a `trailingBuilder` to provide the dropdown action in the parameters, so one is able to customize the trailing widget without need of rebuilding the dropdown action.

title: Suggest final keyword for new Widget classes by default
body:
### Use case

Developers create new widgets frequently. Many of these widgets are not designed for inheritance.

By suggesting final, we prevent accidental subclassing, making the widget safer and signaling the intended use clearly.


Improves code readability and maintainability by indicating immutability at the class level.

Could potentially optimize performance, as Dart may optimize final classes better in certain scenarios.

### Proposal

Currently, when creating a new Widget class in Flutter, IDEs and templates do not suggest using the final keyword for the class. 

Most Widgets are not intended to be subclassed, and marking them as final improves code safety and can have minor performance benefits.


The proposal is to add a suggestion or template update in Flutter tools and IDEs so that when developers create a new Widget, the final keyword is recommended or auto-inserted.

title: Support navigating to source locations from stack frames displayed by `WidgetPreviewErrorWidget`
body: `WidgetPreviewErrorWidget` displays a list of stack frames if a widget throws an exception during initialization. The script URIs for each stack frame should be links that cause connected IDEs to navigate to the specified source location.

title: Allow uniform bindings by name for FragmentShader
Add AlignmentDirectional constants to AlignmentGeometry
body:
### Use case

The `AlignmentGeometry` class now contains redirecting constants like `center` or `topLeft` to allow the usage of dot shorthands:

```dart
Align(
  alignment: .topLeft,
  child: Foo(),
),
```

However, the predefined values for directional alignment (like `topStart`) are missing from the `AlignmentGeometry` class:

```dart
Align(
  // cannot use a dot shorthand
  alignment: AlignmentDirectional.topStart,
  child: Foo(),
),
```

### Proposal

Add the corresponding constants into `AlignmentDirectional` to enable dot shorthands for them as well:

```dart
Align(
  alignment: .topStart,
  child: Foo(),
),
```

title: ☂️[a11y] Use defaultTargetPlatform instead of platform from the theme for Semantics flags in a few widgets
body:
### Use case

I noticed this @chunhtai's comment at https://github.com/flutter/flutter/pull/173418#discussion_r2274331276:

_"we should check defaultTargetPlatform instead of from the theme, if for some reason someone wants android feels and look on an iOS, the voice over will still be broken"_

Indeed, in case users explicitly configure a specific platform (let's give it as Android for eg), but run on another platforms (let's give it as iOS for eg), accessibility might work incorrectly.
There are a few existing widgets that use platform from the theme to check:

- [x] ExpansionTile: https://github.com/flutter/flutter/issues/177002

title: Do we need this "The <parameter_name> parameter is required"?
body:
I see multiple phrases of the form "The <parameter_name> parameter is required" in the doc comments, while it is enforced by the compiler using the required keyword, or the parameter is a regular positional parameter.
For example, in packages/flutter/lib/src/material/menu_anchor.dart:
```dart
  /// The [label] parameter is required.
  const MenuAcceleratorLabel(this.label, {super.key, this.builder = defaultLabelBuilder});
```
Do we need these phrases?

title: Feature request (build): enable engine builds optimized for size
body:
### Use case

As a developer targeting embedded devices, I wish to build the flutter engine optimized for size rather than speed as it is advantageous on some platforms to reduce the binary footprint as much as possible.

### Proposal

Add a flag to the `engine/src/flutter/tools/gn` tool to enable size optimizations, eg

```
/engine/src/flutter/tools/gn --optimize-for-size
```

The flags `--optimize-for-size` and `--unoptimized` should probably be mutually exclusive

title: Add flutter config --ios-min-version to set default iOS deployment target globally
body:
### Use case

Currently, Flutter does not provide a way to set a global default iOS deployment target across all projects. Developers must manually edit each project’s ios/Podfile or the Xcode project settings to change the line:
```
platform :ios, '12.0'
```

This is repetitive and error-prone when maintaining multiple projects or CI/CD environments.

### Proposal

Add a new global configuration option:

`flutter config --ios-min-version 13.0`

This command would:

- Store the specified iOS minimum deployment target in the Flutter config file (~/.flutter_settings or equivalent).
- Apply the version automatically when creating new projects with flutter create.
- Optionally, update existing projects when running flutter pub get or a new command like flutter fix.

**Reason for proposal**

Many plugins (e.g., Firebase, camera, Google Maps) have raised their minimum iOS version to 13.0 or higher.

Developers often maintain multiple Flutter projects; changing each Podfile manually is tedious.
Setting a global default aligns with existing configuration commands such as:

flutter config --android-sdk
flutter config --enable-<feature>

CI/CD environments and team setups would benefit from consistent deployment targets.


Thus, expected behavior is:
```
flutter config --ios-min-version 13.0
flutter create my_app
```
should generate ios/Podfile with:
platform :ios, '13.0'

title: Beta 3.38 blocked by skipped engine build on release branch
body:
Candidate branch: https://github.com/flutter/flutter/tree/flutter-3.38-candidate.0
Dashboard link: https://flutter-dashboard.appspot.com/#/build?repo=flutter&branch=flutter-3.38-candidate.0

https://github.com/flutter/flutter/pull/176746 stamped the new candidate release branch for the 3.38 beta. When this pull request landed, it triggered the `Linux flutter_release_builder`, which [skipped the engine build](https://ci.chromium.org/ui/p/dart-internal/builders/flutter/Linux%20flutter_release_builder/1055/overview) causing  the `Linux flutter_analyze` test to fail when trying to reference the release engine build in https://github.com/flutter/flutter/pull/176799 and postsubmit failures for the stamping PR itself:

- [`Mac_arm64 verify_binaries_codesigned`](https://ci.chromium.org/ui/p/flutter/builders/prod/Mac_arm64%20verify_binaries_codesigned/628/overview)
- [`Mac_x64 verify_binaries_codesigned`](https://ci.chromium.org/ui/p/flutter/builders/prod/Mac_x64%20verify_binaries_codesigned/631/overview)
- [`Linux linux_web_engine_tests`](https://ci.chromium.org/ui/p/flutter/builders/prod/Linux%20linux_web_engine_tests/4050/overview)

We need a way to trigger an engine build for the release and ideally, a way to avoid this problem in the future. 

Currently blocking https://github.com/flutter/flutter/issues/176744.

cc @jtmcdole who mentioned this is likely not related to content-aware hashing but rather another optimization
cc @justinmc

title: Feature request (build): add option to tune for specific CPU
body:
### Use case

As a developer cross-compiling the flutter engine for a specific embedded platform, I want to specify the CPU model in order to get the best performance from the compiled code.

### Proposal

Add a `engine/src/flutter/tools/gn` option `--cpu-tune` or similar that passes a specifc CPU variant through to the compiler's `-mtune` flag.

There is already support for this in the build via the `arm_tune` gn argument in `arm.gni`, for ARM CPUs. It just needs some support from the `gn.py` frontend.

title: Implement RoundedSuperellipseInputBorder
body:
### Use case

I want to use the squircle corners of iOS for my input fields. There is the `RoundedSuperellipseBorder` to do exactly that, but a `TextField` works only with `InputBorder`s that extend from `ShapeBorder`, which makes those classes sibling classes of `OutlinedBorder` which `RoundedSuperellipseBorder` extends from.

### Proposal

I believe there should be a `RoundedSuperellipseInputBorder` that fills the gap. On iOS native text fields use the same corner math, see the search bar in the contacts app, so this is necessary when aiming for platform respective UI.

title: package:web_benchmarks needs to be updated for unified skwasm/canvaskit renderers
body:
Now that the skwasm and canvaskit renderers emit the same frame events (see https://github.com/flutter/flutter/pull/175392), the `expectedBenchmarkMetrics` method here [1] no longer returns the expected events. They are now be the same for both skwasm and canvaskit.

To handle this, I suggest making `useWasm` an optional named parameter instead of required. 

[1] https://github.com/flutter/packages/blob/1c0be204c45eea42927eb6005bb45f16a8fffb07/packages/web_benchmarks/lib/src/metrics.dart#L152-L155

_(Note: This was noticed because the DevTools benchmark tests to began failing, see https://github.com/flutter/devtools/issues/9456)_
