title: drawerEnableOpenDragGesture property in DrawerThemeData
body:
### Use case

I wanted to disable opening app drawer using gesture cause it conflicts with ios back navigation. I then found that scaffold does have the property `drawerEnableOpenDragGesture` for this exact use case. But the issue is that I have a few seperate pages in my applicaiton and in order to disable the drawer gesture in all pages I am required to define this on each scaffold. It's fine and it would work, but I feel that this is code duplication and unncessacary since we already have a pattern to override app wide default values using ThemeData. But when I checked the `DrawerThemeData` it does not have this option.

### Proposal

I would like to have `drawerEnableOpenDragGesture` property in `DrawerThemeData` so that I can change default behaviour across entire application instead of defining it in each scaffold which is code duplication

title: First-party plugin for age range APIs
body:
### Use case

I received this information from Google Play Store

„Since your application is available to the United States, you will have to implement the Play Age Signals API as this will be required by the upcoming laws.“

I could not find any flutter library which helps to implement this. Anybody working on that?

Source: https://support.google.com/googleplay/android-developer/answer/16569691?hl=en

### Proposal

Could this be provided by Flutter directly, or can it be provided by a package on pub.dev/flutter?

title: Expose iOS "Auto-Play Animated Images" accessibility setting
body:
### Use case

In the Zulip app for iOS, we'd like to check whether the iOS 17+ "Auto-Play Animated Images" setting is enabled, so that when loading an image, we can choose between animated and still versions.

- iOS user doc: https://support.apple.com/guide/iphone/reduce-onscreen-motion-iph0b691d3ed/ios
- iOS developer doc: https://developer.apple.com/documentation/swiftui/environmentvalues/accessibilityplayanimatedimages



### Proposal

I propose adding a new field to [`AccessibilityFeatures`](https://api.flutter.dev/flutter/dart-ui/AccessibilityFeatures-class.html), providing this setting's value.

There are already some iOS-only fields, such as [`AccessibilityFeatures.onOffSwitchLabels`](https://api.flutter.dev/flutter/dart-ui/AccessibilityFeatures/onOffSwitchLabels.html).

title: update the material_color_utilities dependency version
body:
### Use case

this doesn't impact flutter directly, but, the current `material_color_utilities` dependency is at `0.11.1` while the latest version on pub.dev is `0.13.0` , this is blocking the use of flutter in other dart projects that depend on `material_color_utilities 0.13.0` and also require flutter as a dependency.
As far as i can tell ... there are no breaking changes so it should be fine to update the dependency without issues.

### Proposal

bump the `material_color_utilities` dependency to version `0.13.0` .

title: Expose iOS "Customize onscreen motion" accessibility settings
body:
_(Replaces #177195, which was about just one setting. This issue covers the whole bundle of related settings.)_

### Use case

In the Zulip app for iOS, we'd like to check whether the iOS 17+ "Auto-Play Animated Images" setting is enabled, so that when loading an image, we can choose between animated and still versions.

- iOS user doc: https://support.apple.com/guide/iphone/reduce-onscreen-motion-iph0b691d3ed/ios
- iOS developer doc: https://developer.apple.com/documentation/swiftui/environmentvalues/accessibilityplayanimatedimages

### Proposal

I propose adding new fields to [`AccessibilityFeatures`](https://api.flutter.dev/flutter/dart-ui/AccessibilityFeatures-class.html), providing "Auto-Play Animated Images" and, for completeness, all the related settings in that [Apple user doc](https://support.apple.com/guide/iphone/reduce-onscreen-motion-iph0b691d3ed/ios):

- [x] Reduce Motion _(already exposed, as [`AccessibilityFeatures.reduceMotion`](https://api.flutter.dev/flutter/dart-ui/AccessibilityFeatures/reduceMotion.html))_
- [ ] Vehicle Motion Cues
- [ ] Dim Flashing Lights
- [ ] Auto-Play Animated Images _(for Zulip's use case; was tracked in #177195)_
- [ ] Auto-Play Video Previews
- [ ] Auto-Play Message Effects
- [ ] Prefer Non-Blinking Cursor
- [ ] Limit Frame Rate

We would follow the existing pattern of iOS-only fields, such as [`AccessibilityFeatures.onOffSwitchLabels`](https://api.flutter.dev/flutter/dart-ui/AccessibilityFeatures/onOffSwitchLabels.html), by saying in the dartdocs "Only supported on iOS."

title: Improve error message on string matching
body: We should improve the error message on string matching in flutter_test. We have been looking at how to best roll in updates to date localizations that have (in a few cases) updated typical whitespace (U+0020) to be NBSP (non breaking space, U+202F). The localizations team confirmed this is WAI, and in some locales (like French) is a required typography rule. We have found users' might experience this update like this: Which would be super frustrating to debug! We should improve the error message here to specify what the whitespace character is in the error message. Separately we also discussed that in some cases (maybe not this particular case) users might like a test method that allows them to match strings without attention paid to whitespace, tracked in https://github.com/flutter/flutter/issues/177478 A little bit more context on this from the localizations team is available in b/442629033

title: [Flutter web-semantics] Feature request: Enable Accessibility button upper left 1 pixel instead of offscreen 1 pixel
body:
### Use case

Automation tools such as Maestro and probably Appium seem to not be able to tap on
`      document.querySelector('flt-semantics-placeholder[aria-label="Enable accessibility"]')
                .click();` because it is at absolute positivion -1 left and -1 top of the screen (offscreen).

Maestro does appear to be able to tap on an invisible button with no text if it is at 1,1.

I am not sure if i have bandwidth to PR this but i wanted to get the discussion going.
See: https://docs.flutter.dev/ui/accessibility/web-accessibility#invisible-button

Can anyone think of a reason that the ('flt-semantics-placeholder[aria-label="Enable accessibility"]' has to be offscreen rather than "just" onepixel, invisible, transparent etc?


### Proposal

Change just 2 characters in the code to make the button be "+" 1 left and "+" one top insteda of "-" (or remove the negative)

title: [pigeon] Consider generating singletons for @EventChannelApi
body:
### Use case

Hello, I love how pigeon deals with the complexity of native channels, however, due to my unfamiliarity with the topic I made a mistake of calling the generated method for creating an EventChannel stream, thus breaking platform communication with previous instances. I understand that this might be a problem that some Plugin code must deal with, but this also seems a pretty general problem. So, consider generating code that will ensure only one instance of the EventChannel exists. I am currently using this pattern that I found in a plugin that I use:

https://github.com/llfbandit/app_links/blob/e99efa708dde9886e0982d9b53b7b6b05e807c31/app_links/lib/src/app_links.dart#L52-L86

Here a StreamController is used that encapsulates the EventChannel stream and makes sure only one exists and also closing it when no subscribers are left.


### Proposal

I propose that the class that is annotated with `@EventChannelApi()` is used as the singleton class name and that methods or getters in this class create the needed StreamControllers.

title: Migrate Expansible animation properties to AnimationStyle for a less broad API surface
body:
### Use case

[Expansible](https://api.flutter.dev/flutter/widgets/Expansible-class.html) has animation properties [Expansible.curve](https://api.flutter.dev/flutter/widgets/Expansible/curve.html), [Expansible.reverseCurve](https://api.flutter.dev/flutter/widgets/Expansible/reverseCurve.html) and [Expansible.duration](https://api.flutter.dev/flutter/widgets/Expansible/duration.html). These properties are encapsulated in [AnimationStyle](https://api.flutter.dev/flutter/animation/AnimationStyle-class.html).

### Proposal

Deprecate these properties and replace them with a single `animationStyle` property.

title: Remove redundant codecs on Android

title: macOS flutter_framework_uncompressed_bytes regression related to Dart SDK roll
body:
The `hello_world_macos__compile`/`flutter_framework_uncompressed_bytes` metric has increased by 445kb (1.5%) starting with the Dart roll at https://github.com/flutter/flutter/commit/376a0b25d8f6a22a5a763a082e193b3ca061dcb8

Confirmed locally by running a `flutter build macos` release app build in https://github.com/flutter/flutter/tree/master/examples/hello_world.  The size of the `FlutterMacOS` binary within `FlutterMacOS.framework` is larger at that commit.

Performance dashboard link:
https://flutter-flutter-perf.luci.app/e/?begin=1760857526&enable_chart_tooltip=true&end=1762186209&keys=X4129c1b014a53ca2bc7747c174d61ea5&plotSummary=true&use_titles=true&xbaroffset=2804913&graph=0&commit=2804909&trace=2&request_type=0

title: Make generic paths look better on platforms without MSAA.
body:
### Use case

On platforms without MSAA, the tessellated paths we render look very choppy (see https://github.com/flutter/flutter/issues/167181).

### Proposal

This can be alleviated by using SDFs to render generic paths. We've done the same with circles (https://github.com/flutter/flutter/pull/177482) and lines (https://github.com/flutter/flutter/pull/164734). Any other solution that makes generic paths look nicer will also do.

This will require changes to `Canvas::DrawPath` https://github.com/flutter/flutter/blob/e5d5c01850f2d33106f00a19017682f09e83e48b/engine/src/flutter/impeller/display_list/canvas.cc#L327

title: Allow DropdownMenu to be non-editable and focusable (select control)
body:
### Use case A `DropdownMenu` configured as a non-editable dropdown (select control) should not allow text input in its text field, regardless of how it receives focus. When focused, the component as a whole should be highlighted, allowing the user to open the menu with keyboard actions such as the `Space` or `Enter` key. This behavior would align with standard dropdown components, such as the [Material Web select component](https://material-web.dev/components/select/#usage). Right now, it is not possible to have a non-editable dropdown and also provide a `FocusNode`. To clarify, by non-editable dropdown I mean a dropdown that works like a normal select control without a text field, only a selector. In many real situations we still need to use a `FocusNode` object, for example: 1. When submitting the previous field and programmatically moving focus to the dropdown. 2. When submitting a form and focusing the first invalid field, which might be the dropdown. --- **Example of current behavior:**
Details To create a `DropdownMenu` that functions as a simple, non-editable selector, the current approach involves setting `requestFocusOnTap: false`, `enableSearch: false`, and `enableFilter: false`. While this correctly prevents the text field from activating on a mouse click, it does not prevent keyboard input when the widget gains focus through other means. In this example, pressing the button gives focus to the `DropdownMenu`, which incorrectly allows text input. Flutter version: ```text stable : 3.35.7 master : 3.38.0-1.0.pre-407 ``` ```dart import 'package:flutter/material.dart'; void main() => runApp(const MyApp()); class MyApp extends StatefulWidget { const MyApp({super.key}); @override State createState() => _MyAppState(); } class _MyAppState extends State { @override Widget build(BuildContext context) { return MaterialApp( home: Scaffold( body: Container( padding: const EdgeInsets.all(24.0), alignment: Alignment.center, child: Column( mainAxisAlignment: MainAxisAlignment.center, spacing: 24, children: [ OutlinedButton( onPressed: () => focusNode.requestFocus(), child: const Text('Focus DropdownMenu'), ), DropdownMenu
### Proposal #### Proposal 1 If [enableFilter](https://main-api.flutter.dev/flutter/material/DropdownMenu/enableFilter.html) is set to `false` and [enableSearch](https://main-api.flutter.dev/flutter/material/DropdownMenu/enableSearch.html) is also set to `false`, the widget should behave like a select control without a text field, regardless of how focus is requested or whether a custom `FocusNode` is provided. It should not depend on `requestFocusOnTap` for that behavior. The [requestFocusOnTap](https://main-api.flutter.dev/flutter/material/DropdownMenu/requestFocusOnTap.html) parameter should be removed. --- Keep in mind that if we choose this proposal, we must also consider another scenario that is currently not implemented: **When `DropdownMenu` is used as a select, it should focus/highlight the item that matches keyboard input even when there is no text field.** This matters because the current [enableSearch](https://main-api.flutter.dev/flutter/material/DropdownMenu/enableSearch.html) setting disables highlight behavior when set to `false`, which could conflict with this new behavior if implemented in the future.
Example **Case 1** 1. Open dropdown 2. Press **"a"** (Go to the first item that starts with "a") 3. Press **"a"** again (Go to the next item that starts with "a") 4. Press **"a"** again (Return to the first item that starts with "a" since there are no more matches) **Case 2** 2. Quickly type a combination of keys like `"a4"` — this focuses the item that matches that prefix. In this case it jumps to `a4` and skips `a2` and `a3`.
--- #### Proposal 2 Add another parameter to make the component work strictly as a select. Possible names: * `showSearch`: `bool` * `searchable` : `bool` * `selectOnly`: `bool` * `enableTextFieldSearch`: `bool` * `mode`: `enum DropdownMenuMode {select, textField}` The [requestFocusOnTap](https://main-api.flutter.dev/flutter/material/DropdownMenu/requestFocusOnTap.html) parameter should be removed. In this case, make sure the current [enableSearch](https://main-api.flutter.dev/flutter/material/DropdownMenu/enableSearch.html) does not cause confusion with this new parameter.

title: Tree Red: Missing dependencies on CI Ubuntu 22
body:
In order to address the TASK_BOT died errors, we downgraded from Ubuntu 24 to Ubuntu 22. This rolled out slowly on Nov 4th and uncovered some test failures on the dashboard. These failures appear around many of the Android tests which do not include ninja / cmake / clang in their dependencies list of ci.yaml.

Updating multiple locations in: #178054 

```
          {"dependency": "clang", "version": "git_revision:5d5aba78dbbee75508f01bcaa69aedb2ab79065a"},
          {"dependency": "cmake", "version": "build_id:8787856497187628321"},
          {"dependency": "ninja", "version": "version:1.9.0"}
```

This has the tools installed in the cache on the CI machines and appear to fix the issue.

Outstanding issue: [Linux web_skwasm_tests_2](https://ci.chromium.org/ui/p/flutter/builders/try/Linux%20web_skwasm_tests_2) appears to be failing with a chrome conneciton.

title: Please collect anonymized build-time histograms
body: Currently the `flutter` tool does not collect build-time data (afaict). Based on my conversations with Shorebird customers and some minimal (anonymized) histogram sharing from at least one popular flutter build provider, long-tail build times in the wild are much worse than I realized. We had one customer who has had (for months!) 3hour flutter build times. We have another who reports 2 hour flutter build times. These are outliers, I'm sure. But we don't know how bad it is because we don't have good data. Google is uniquely positioned to collect (and respond to) some data here. Here is an anonymized histogram from this provider earlier this year. This was just a tiny sample of their builds, so I'm sure it's not fully representative. But even this, shows a very long tail distribution. My guess is that this is maybe ~1d of work in the Flutter tool. Sharing this kind of data (bucketed total time of execution) is something we already report via dart analytics for the analyzer, and should be well within existing analytics agreements. The only work here should be actually making the change to flutter_tool. I'd recommend reporting bucketed-by-minute. If you'd like to see information about what Chrome does (who has 1000+ analytics like this), they have lots of docs: https://chromium.googlesource.com/chromium/src/+/lkgr/tools/metrics/histograms/README.md

title: Improve navigation order for Screenreaders when using Slivers to serve Material Guidelines
body:
### Use case

[According to Material Design, the **focus order of elements** in a Scaffold should be AppBar -> Navigation Bar -> FAB -> Content](https://m3.material.io/components/floating-action-button/accessibility#c9dfe413-7661-43f5-988b-bce6da2bc47e)

This is easy to do as long **as your AppBar is in the Scaffold's appbar slot as now appBar, body and fab are siblings**. You can just set the semantics sortKey and the screenreader will navigate accordingly.

However, when it comes to slivers, the AppBar is part of the body's CustomScrollView.
You will have to scroll through the entire body to get to the fab. That's very bad user experience. Maybe I am missing something, because this seems to be quite a fundamental problem with how sliver pages are built.

There shouldn't be the need for some hacky solution to this.

```Scaffold(
      body: CustomScrollView(
        slivers: [
          SliverAppBar(
            title: const Text('My Title'),
          ),
          SliverList(
            delegate: SliverChildBuilderDelegate(
                  (context, index) => ListTile(
                    title: Text('Entry $index'),
                  ),
              childCount: 20,
            ),
          ),
        ],
      ),
      floatingActionButton: FloatingActionButton(
        onPressed: () {},
        tooltip: "Add To List",
        child: const Icon(Icons.add),
      ),
    );
```

### Proposal

The best solution would be to be able to set the order across the entire screen and not just siblings.

title: [tool] Deprecations validator truncates its error message right before the required "v" prefix of the deprecation version tag
body:
For https://github.com/flutter/flutter/pull/178242 I was looking to add a new deprecation.
I looked at the docs in https://github.com/flutter/flutter/blob/master/docs/contributing/Tree-hygiene.md#deprecations to see how the deprecation message is structured and started working on my PR.

However, when running `./dev/bots/analyze.dart` I noticed that the deprecations validator truncates its error message just before the `v` of the version, which made it less obvious in the error message. I had taken the template from the docs as verbatim, but overlooked a typo.

### Proposal

1) In https://github.com/flutter/flutter/blob/master/docs/contributing/Tree-hygiene.md#deprecations
 change the template to the following, since I presume that more people might take the template as verbatim?

```
@Deprecated(
  '[description of how to migrate] '
  '[brief motivation for why we are breaking the API] '
  'This feature was deprecated after v[beta version at time of deprecation].'
)
```

2) In https://github.com/flutter/flutter/blob/master/dev/bots/analyze.dart#L762-L764 don't truncate the message before the leading "v", as the regex in https://github.com/flutter/flutter/blob/master/dev/bots/analyze.dart#L716-L718 specifically requires it. Perhaps the message should be updated to use a version pattern, instead of three periods?

title: Android's predictive back feature doesn't use the device's corner radius
body: Since predictive back is now enabled by default, it would be better to have it polished and match the native behaviour, currently it uses a fixed border radius when the effect is active, which looks bad, Android provided APIs that get the devices corner radius which can be used to achieve a more seamless effect and match the native side. Current implementation: in my custom implementation, all I did was use a ClipRRect on a whole page and set the corner radius to the device's using this package [corner_radius_plugin](https://pub.dev/packages/corner_radius_plugin), it uses official Android APIs to get the devices corner radius. the result is a more native behavior:

title: Offer a convenient way of handling image paste events in flutter web
body:
### Use case

Imagine you build a chat app for flutter web. For desktop users, it is normal and expected, to copy a file from their file browser, enter the chat app, and press CMD+V (STRG+V) to paste the image into the application.
This is supported in many popular web apps that have rich inputs, such as WhatsApp, Teams, GitHub, and thousands more. While writing this issue, I could press CMD+V to attach an image that is in my clipboard.

I really struggled to find a way to do this with Flutter Web.
In the end, it was possible by using web.dart and creating a JavaScript event listener (and then abstracting it so the code is executed only on web platforms).
But this is overly complicated and does not feel stable; native support would be better.

### Proposal

Create a nice and clean API for accepting pasted files. E.g.
```dart
    return OnFilePasteListener(
      (file) => doSomething(),
      child: child,
    );
```

title: Flutter web proxy serve static files
body:
### Use case

I have an existing proxy serving a few static directories. For example: https://localhost:port/app1/#. The applications need to run on the same port for cookies to work. I want to keep only the Flutter web proxy, but it needs to support serving static files.

### Proposal

I propose this configuration:
```yaml
server:
  host: "localhost"
  port: 3000
  proxy:
  - directory: "../app/static/"
  - prefix: "/app"
  - spa: true
```
"prefix" already exists, "directory" would be the static files path and "spa" would return index.html when the file is not found.

title: showCupertinoSheet is missing RouteSettings
body:
### Use case

Almost all navigation APIs in Flutter have an optional `RouteSettings` argument, including `showDialog`, `MaterialPageRoute`, `CupertinoPageRoute`, and even popup menu buttons like `PopupMenuButton`.

`showCupertinoSheet` does *not* have a RouteSettings, making it very difficult to trace - especially given the [recent fixes](https://github.com/flutter/packages/pull/9436) in GoRouter, where we can now observe almost all navigation.

### Proposal

Add a `RouteSettings?: routeSettings` to `showCupertinoSheet`

title: Caching for both Flutter/native and over several Flutter Engines
body:
### Use case

Problem: Our team requires a lot of caching as our users frequently run in to issues with low connectivity. We would like to have a common cache between Flutter and native. We would also like to have a persistent cache between Flutter engines.

Alternative Solution: Use cached_network_image which uses flutter_cache_manager. This solution isn't being kept up to date, not having been updated in over a year, as well as having many defects.

### Proposal

Create a Flutter first-party package that handles caching on disk so that it can be persisted for a Flutter/native solution and a solution over multiple Flutter engines. Since images are one of the most common uses, a simple implementation for caching images over the network would be ideal.

title: Add a way to clear the _errorText.value in FormFieldState without having to call .reset()
body:
### Use case

For a custom Form, if we have a field showing an error after calling `validate()`, it could be required that modifying a different field changes the state of the Form, including the first invalid one.
If we use the auto validate mode, the field will be still invalid, but we may not want to show that to the user until they make a different action (like pressing a button).

This can be somewhat accomplished by calling `FormState.reset()`  and then `didChange(previousValue)` but it seems more like a workaround than the right way of just clearing the error.

### Proposal

To add a new method `FormState.clearError()` which would just set the `_errorText.value` to `null`, similar to the `reset()` method without modifying the field value.

title: [Linux] Use `Adwaita Sans` over `Cantarell` in `_helsinkiFontFallbacks`
body:
### Use case

Gnome* 48 (from March 2025) switched its default font from Cantarell to Adwaita Sans: https://blogs.gnome.org/monster/introducing-adwaita-fonts/.

Flutter likewise should switch to use Adwaita Sans if available in its font fallbacks.

Adwaita Sans is a slightly tweaked version of Inter, and therefore fits in with `Roboto` and the other Linux fonts listed in `_helsinkiFontFallbacks`.

*For context, Gnome is one of the two most popular desktop environments on Linux, and is used on Ubuntu.

### Proposal

```patch
   static const List<String> _helsinkiFontFallbacks = <String>[
     'Ubuntu',
+    'Adwaita Sans',
     'Cantarell',
     'DejaVu Sans',
     'Liberation Sans',
     'Arial',
   ];
```

Adwaita Sans should be preferred over Cantarell, but the Ubuntu font should be preferred over either.

title: Calling setFlashMode on devices without flash mode support throws an exception which prevents correct initialization of the CameraController.
body:
### Use case

Description:
1.) `flashMode` is set as default to `FlashMode.auto`.
2.) The function `setFlashMode` (line 697) tries to set the flash mode to `auto` by `CameraPlatform.instance.setFlashMode(_cameraId, mode)`.

https://github.com/flutter/packages/blob/c8be05d56bc7bdf28b99e0d28caf2d28bb8ac0e8/packages/camera/camera/lib/src/camera_controller.dart#L697

3.) On devices without flash mode support, an exception is thrown (line 684: throw CameraException).
https://github.com/flutter/packages/blob/c8be05d56bc7bdf28b99e0d28caf2d28bb8ac0e8/packages/camera/camera/lib/src/camera_controller.dart#L684

4.) Because of this `CameraException`, the initialization of the CameraController fails.

Solution:
1.) Remove `throw CameraException` in 684.
2.) Add `value = value.copyWith(flashMode: FlashMode.off);` in line 684.
https://github.com/flutter/packages/blob/c8be05d56bc7bdf28b99e0d28caf2d28bb8ac0e8/packages/camera/camera/lib/src/camera_controller.dart#L684



### Proposal

Enhancement:
To give other packages (which are using the camera package) a hint that flash mode is set to off because there is no flash mode support, a variable like `hasFlashModeSupport` could be added in line 122.
https://github.com/flutter/packages/blob/c8be05d56bc7bdf28b99e0d28caf2d28bb8ac0e8/packages/camera/camera/lib/src/camera_controller.dart#L122

And `hasFlashModeSupport` could be set to `false` in line 685.

title: winit-wgpu based shell
body:
### Use case
winit-wgpu based shell
___
- [winit](https://github.com/rust-windowing/winit) is a cross-platform window manager written in Rust
- [wgpu](https://github.com/gfx-rs/wgpu) is a cross-platform gpu abstraction layer (something like [ANGLE](https://github.com/google/angle) or [Qt's RHI](https://doc.qt.io/qt-6/qrhi.html)) written In Rust.

### Pros
- minimize the platform specific code needed to be written by plugin creators.
- less burden maintaining shells for each platform (there would just be one shell [ideally] for all platforms)
- opens an opportunity to integrate with the vast rust eco-system for gpu related things.
### Cons
- Platform widgets are probably not a thing but 
   - anyways they are not supported on desktop 
   - the community is encoureged to write plugins that would work in the **same** way across all platforms
so things like media players from the native platform wouldn't be required. in-app browsers can use something like [servo](https://servo.org/) map-gis widgets can use something like [galileo](https://github.com/galileo-map/galileo) 
- wgpu is an abstraction layer, so performance are always subpar to native api's although I don't know the deviation.
- Accessibility, theoretically possible but not familiar with it. https://github.com/AccessKit/accesskit

see also https://github.com/anlumo/flutter_embedder that experimented with the idea.

title: Enable support for KHR_Debug in Impeller GLES Playground unit tests
body:
When running the Playground Impeller unit tests on the OpenGLES backend, it would be useful to have support for [KHR_Debug](https://registry.khronos.org/OpenGL/extensions/KHR/KHR_debug.txt) in Debug mode to get enhanced OpenGL Diagnostics.

I propose the following - 

* Have GLFW create a [Debug Context](https://www.glfw.org/docs/latest/window_guide.html#window_hints_ctx) when running in debug mode
* Add support for glDebugMessageCallback in the GLFW backend to log OpenGL diagnostics

title: Add showValueIndicator to Slider widget.
body:
### Use case

It is possible to set the conditions under which the value indicator on a `Slider` will be shown by setting `showValueIndicator` in `SliderTheme`. However, `Slider` itself is lacking the `showValueIndicator` property. 

When a user wants to override the `showValueIndicator` for a specific `Slider`, they have to wrap it into `SliderTheme` to achieve the desired behavior.

```dart
SliderTheme(
  data: SliderThemeData(
    showValueIndicator: ShowValueIndicator.alwaysVisible,
  ),
  child: Slider(
    value: 0.5,
    label: '0.5',
    onChanged: (double value) {},
  ),
),
```

### Proposal

Add `Slider.showValueIndicator` property to allow user to set the conditions under which the value indicator will be shown for a specific `Slider`.

```
Slider(
  value: 0.5,
  label: '0.5',
  onChanged: (double value) {},
  mouseCursor: SystemMouseCursors.click,
  showValueIndicator: ShowValueIndicator.alwaysVisible,
)
```

widget_preview_scaffold blocking packages_autoroller
body:
`flutter_tools` was the only package to depend on `unified_analytics`. During the autoroll, there is a mismatch in versions of this package:


```
Resolving dependencies in `packages/flutter_tools/test/widget_preview_scaffold.shard/widget_preview_scaffold`...
Because every version of flutter_tools from path depends on unified_analytics 8.0.10 and widget_preview_scaffold depends on unified_analytics 8.0.5, flutter_tools from path is forbidden.
So, because widget_preview_scaffold depends on flutter_tools from path, version solving failed.
```

title: Missing SnackBarTheme
Add .toolTip property to PlatformMenuBar's PlatformMenuItem class
body:
### Use case

When writing MacOS applications, one is forced to use `PlatformMenuBar` and related classes to interact with the native MacOS menu system. 

PlatformMenuBar's `PlatformMenuItem` class has no way to specify a tooltip for menu items . This is extremely common on MacOS (e.g. the "Open Recent" menu items in BBEdit, etc).

Both native MacOS and the for-Windows-platform `MenuBar` widget allow addition of tooltips to menu items. 

Thus Mac platform flutter applications lack a capability that both flutter-on-windows and native-macos applications have. 

**Windows**

The Windows alternative to PlatformMenuBar, the flutter SDK `MenuBar` widget, allows easy addition of tooltips for menus using a `Tooltip` widget: 

```
MenuItemButton(
  child: Tooltip(
    message: 'Open an existing file',
    child: Text('Open'),
  ),
```

**Native MacOS**

In native MacOS, tooltips can be added easily by using the `.toolTip` property of `NSMenuItem` as documented at: https://developer.apple.com/documentation/appkit/nsmenuitem

**Flutter-on-MacOS**

No capability exists to specify a tooltip for a menu item. See: https://api.flutter.dev/flutter/widgets/PlatformMenuItem-class.html

Only a String .label can be set. 

### Proposal


Feature parity with flutter-on-windows and native-macos could be accomplished by:

- adding a `.toolTip` parameter to flutter's `PlatformMenuItem` widget
- including that parameter in the serialization of data to pass through the native bridge
- including that parameter in de-serialization of data in macOS native code
- applying the parameter when creating `NSMenuItem` instances

`PlatformMenuBar` uses the MacOS `NSMenuItem` class directly for actual native implementation. (See: https://github.com/flutter/flutter/blob/master/engine/src/flutter/shell/platform/darwin/macos/framework/Source/FlutterMenuPlugin.mm)

`NSMenuItem` already accepts an optional string for `.toolTip` (which flutter does not use). 

All the pieces are there, it just needs an expansion of the parameter set from Dart code through to the `NSMenuItem` object instantiation.

title: [flutter_test] Add a Matcher for CapturedAccessibilityAnnouncement
body:
### Use case

Currently, when testing announceSemantics, we retrieve a list of `CapturedAccessibilityAnnouncement` objects via `tester.takeAnnouncements()`. Verifying the content of these announcements often requires manual checks of the properties (`message`, `textDirection`, `assertiveness`), which can be verbose, especially for lists.

### Proposal

I suggest introducing an `accessibilityAnnouncement` matcher (with PR #180058). This would allow developers to write cleaner and more readable tests.


### Benefits

The main advantage is the ability to easily check **lists of announcements** using standard collection matchers.

For example, existing tests in `widget_tester_test.dart` or user apps often manually iterate through the list or check indices. With this matcher, we can simply use `contains`, `orderedEquals`, or `containsAll`.

**Example Usage:**

*Current approach:*

```dart
expect(tester.takeAnnouncements().length, 1);
expect(tester.takeAnnouncements().first.message, 'Hello');
expect(tester.takeAnnouncements().first.textDirection, TextDirection.ltr);

```

*With proposed Matcher:*

```dart
expect(
  tester.takeAnnouncements(),
  [accessibilityAnnouncement('Hello', textDirection: TextDirection.ltr)],
);

```

title: [plugin_platform_interface] Improve documentation regarding the relationship between ffigen/jnigen and federated packages.
body:
### Use case

Considering jni and jnigen provide access to the android activity and context, interacting with platform APIs like MediaRouter and Exoplayer no longer requires the extension of FlutterPlugin. However, there is limited documentation on how to create federated plugins with platform code that do not extend FlutterPlugin.

### Proposal

Add documentation or an example demonstrating best practices for creating a federated plugin that exposes jni/ffi bindings without extending FlutterPlugin on the platform side.

title: `RadioListTile`, `CheckboxListTile`, and `SwitchListTile` should accept a `WidgetStatesController` for their backing `ListTile`
body:
### Use case

I would like to listen to the state of a `RadioListTile`, `CheckboxListTile` or `SwitchListTile` (e.g., `pressed` or `hovered`) using a `WidgetStatesController`

### Proposal

Add a `statesController` parameter to `RadioListTile`, `CheckboxListTile`, and `SwitchListTile`, which will be forwarded to the `ListTile`.

title: Logs from `dart:developer` are not displayed in the terminal upon running the app with `flutter run --debug`
body:
### Steps to reproduce

1. Launch a Flutter app in terminal via `flutter run --debug` 
2. Try to log something with `dart:developer.log` and `print` functions.
3. Only a log from `print` function will be displayed in the terminal.

Yes, I know that logs from `dart:developer.log` are available in the DevTools and IDE Run/Debug consoles, but it would be convenient to see them in the terminal as well.

`print`/`debugPrint` methods won't work in my use-case, because the functions truncate long text. Moreover, they don't provide a way to supply `Level` to the logs to filter them in the DevTools unlike `dart:developer.log`. 

Related issue: https://github.com/flutter/flutter/issues/22665

### Expected results

`dart:developer.log` does not output logs in the terminal.

### Actual results

`dart:developer.log` displays logs in the terminal.

### Code sample

<details open><summary>Code sample</summary>

```dart
print('Hello world');
dart_developer.log('Hello World from dart:developer');
```

</details>

</details>

title: Windows template utils.cpp: Use wcsnlen instead of wcslen for defensive programming (CWE-126)
body:
## Description

The Windows runner template file `packages/flutter_tools/templates/app/windows.tmpl/runner/utils.cpp` uses `wcslen` at line 52, which doesn't handle non-null-terminated strings. While the current usage is safe (input comes from Windows APIs that guarantee null-termination), using `wcsnlen` would be better defensive programming and resolves security scanner warnings (CWE-126: Buffer Over-read).

## Current Code (line 52)

```cpp
int input_length = (int)wcslen(utf16_string);
```

## Proposed Fix

```cpp
int input_length = (int)wcsnlen(utf16_string, 32767);
```

## Why This Matters

1. **Security Scanners**: Tools like Semgrep/GitLab SAST flag this as HIGH severity (CWE-126)
2. **Defensive Programming**: Even though current usage is safe, `wcsnlen` provides an extra safety bound
3. **No Performance Impact**: `wcsnlen` has negligible overhead for typical string lengths
4. **Consistency**: Aligns with Microsoft's secure CRT recommendations

## Related

- Related to #134227 (Signed/Unsigned mismatch in same file)
- [CWE-126: Buffer Over-read](https://cwe.mitre.org/data/definitions/126.html)
- [Microsoft strnlen documentation](https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/cpp/c-runtime-library/reference/strnlen-strnlen-s)

## Risk Assessment

**LOW** - This is a minor defensive improvement. The existing code is functionally correct because:
- Input strings come from `CommandLineToArgvW` which guarantees null-termination
- The null check on line 45 handles the nullptr case

However, the fix follows security best practices and silences legitimate scanner warnings.

## Checklist

- [x] I have read the [contributor guide](https://github.com/flutter/flutter/blob/main/CONTRIBUTING.md)
- [x] I have an issue that this PR addresses
- [x] All tests pass locally

title: Rename "widgetTester" parameter to "tester" in "WidgetTesterCallback"
body:
### Use case

The `WidgetTesterCallback` typedef currently names its parameter `widgetTester`.

In practice, Flutter documentation, tutorials, and the vast majority of real-world
test code use the shorter and idiomatic name `tester`. Because IDEs use the
typedef’s parameter name for autocomplete, developers frequently need to manually
rename `widgetTester` to `tester` when writing widget tests.

This creates minor but repeated friction and inconsistency with established
Flutter conventions. There is no functional issue, but it negatively impacts
developer experience and API consistency.


### Proposal

Rename the parameter in the `WidgetTesterCallback` typedef from `widgetTester`
to `tester`.

Current:

```dart
typedef WidgetTesterCallback = Future<void> Function(WidgetTester widgetTester);
```

Proposed:

```dart
typedef WidgetTesterCallback = Future<void> Function(WidgetTester tester);

```

This change is non-breaking, as parameter names in function typedefs are not part
of the public API contract.

title: [Proposal] Create naming conventions for partial & exact matchers
body:
### Proposal

Create the following conventions for matcher prefixes:

1. **Partial matchers**. Example: `isPerson(name: 'Loic')`.

   A partial matcher only matches the properties you provide. The matcher's arguments are `null` by default.

   You cannot use a partial matcher to assert a value is `null`. For example, I cannot check that a person's nickname is `null` using: `isPerson(name: 'Loic', nickname: null)`. Instead, you need to use:

  ```dart
  expect(person, isPerson(name: 'Loic'));
  expect(person.nickname, isNull);
  ```

2. **Exact matchers**. Example: `matchesPerson(name: 'Loic', nickname: null)`.

   An exact matcher matches all the values, including those you omit. The matcher's arguments have the same default value as the object's default value.

   The exact matcher is often less convenient than the partial matcher. To match an object with 20 non-default property values, you'll need to provide all 20 values to the exact matcher.

### Migration

To adopt this naming convention, we'll want to:

1. Codify this to our style guide
2. Deprecate [`containsSemantics`](https://api.flutter.dev/flutter/flutter_test/containsSemantics.html) in favor of a new `isSemantics` matcher. (For history behind `containsSemantics`, see: https://github.com/flutter/flutter/issues/107859)

cc @chunhtai

title: Add a semanticsLabel for the BottomNavigationBarItem
body:
### Use case

Currently, BottomNavigationBarItem uses the label property for both visual display and accessibility (screen readers) in the BottomNavigationBar widget. This creates a problem when we need to provide additional context for screen reader users.

### Proposal

I already have an [associated PR](https://github.com/flutter/flutter/pull/180577/files) that adds a String semanticsLabel property to BottomNavigationBarItem

An alternative approach could be to replace the current String label with a Widget label, giving developers full control over both the visual content and its accessibility semantics. However, this would be a breaking change.

Happy to adjust the implementation based on your feedback.

title: [webview_flutter_android] Expose an option to receive onNavigationRequest even when isMainFrame == false
body:
In webview_flutter_android, onNavigationRequest is currently only triggered when isMainFrame == true.

(this change from https://github.com/flutter/packages/pull/6335, it should have an option)

This makes it impossible to intercept or observe navigations that occur in subframes (e.g. iframes), such as:
	•	OAuth / SSO flows embedded in iframes
	•	Payment providers loading redirect pages inside subframes
	•	Tracking / verification pages relying on iframe navigation

On Android WebView, shouldOverrideUrlLoading does provide subframe callbacks, but Flutter users cannot access them due to this hard restriction.

Current behavior
	•	onNavigationRequest is silently skipped when isMainFrame == false
	•	No configuration or opt-in mechanism is provided
	•	Developers cannot build consistent navigation interception logic across platforms

Expected behavior
Expose an opt-in option that allows developers to receive onNavigationRequest callbacks even when isMainFrame == false, for example:
	•	A new flag on NavigationDelegate, such as:
```
NavigationDelegate(
  onNavigationRequest: ...,
  includeSubframes: true,
)
```

title: Add FragmentProgram.fromBytes / fromBuffer to Support Dynamically Loading Compiled Runtime Shaders
body:
### Use case

Today `dart:ui.FragmentProgram` can only be created via `FragmentProgram.fromAsset(String assetKey)`, which requires the shader to be packaged in the app’s AssetBundle. This works well for static shaders, but it makes it harder to support some common workflows for apps that rely heavily on runtime shaders, especially multi-pass / render-graph style effects.

Examples:

- **Remote build service / CI pipeline**: compile `.frag` (with includes) on a server into the engine’s compiled format (e.g. Impeller `.iplr`), then deliver the compiled result to the app.
- **A/B testing / feature flags**: roll out shader variants (quality/performance tradeoffs) without repackaging the app.
- **Online shader libraries / UGC**: load compiled shader effects on demand (e.g. from a catalog) rather than shipping everything as assets.
- **Developer tooling**: faster iteration by pulling compiled shader blobs from a local server instead of rebuilding the asset bundle.

Alternative solutions considered:

- **Pre-bundling many variants as assets**: does not scale and still requires app updates.
- **Engine fork**: possible but high maintenance cost.
- **Hot reload / VM service**: helpful for development only, not a production solution.
- **A package on pub.dev** cannot fully solve this because `FragmentProgram` construction is in `dart:ui`, and currently only supports loading from assets.

---

### Proposal

Add a public API to create a `FragmentProgram` from in-memory compiled shader bytes (or an `ImmutableBuffer`), enabling dynamic loading of *compiled* runtime shaders at runtime.

Possible API shapes:

```dart
// Option A: bytes directly
static Future<FragmentProgram> fromBytes(
  Uint8List bytes, {
  String? debugName,
});
```

```dart
// Option B: align with ImmutableBuffer patterns
static Future<FragmentProgram> fromBuffer(
  ImmutableBuffer buffer, {
  String? debugName,
});
```

Notes / expected behavior:

- The API accepts **compiled shader blobs** (e.g. `.iplr` produced by `impellerc`), not shader source.
- If the current platform/backend cannot consume the provided format, the API should throw a clear, actionable error.
- Consider caching semantics similar to `fromAsset` to avoid repeated initialization; optionally allow callers to provide a cache key.

This feature would unlock remote compilation workflows and dynamic delivery of shader effects without requiring an engine fork.

title: Officially supported approach for low-level GPU / OpenGL / custom rendering in Flutter?
body:
### Use case

Hello Flutter team,

I am building a cross-platform application that **requires low-level GPU rendering** (OpenGL/WebGL/Vulkan-like control, custom shaders, offscreen rendering, native textures, etc.).

Currently, there is no officially supported API for this in Flutter.  
Community packages such as `flutter_gl`, 'flutter_opengl', 'flutter_angle', 'openworld_gl' attempt to fill this gap, but in practice they:
- are often outdated,
- break with newer Flutter versions,
- require manual patching to work at all,
- rely on unstable or undocumented engine behavior.

This makes it extremely difficult to build and maintain serious graphics-heavy applications on top of Flutter.

My questions are:

1. Is Flutter intentionally designed to not support low-level graphics access (e.g. OpenGL/Vulkan/WebGPU)?
2. Is there any officially recommended approach for advanced custom rendering beyond CustomPainter and Skia?
3. Is there a public roadmap regarding:
   - official GPU APIs,
   - native texture workflows,
   - external rendering engines embedding,
   - or deeper access to the rendering pipeline?

To be clear, I am not asking for a third-party package recommendation.  
I am trying to understand whether this use case is fundamentally outside of Flutter’s design goals, or whether there is an intended long-term direction for it.

Thank you for your time and for the work on Flutter.

### Proposal

Flutter should provide an officially supported, stable way to perform low-level GPU rendering and integrate external rendering engines.

Specifically, the platform should support one or more of the following (officially documented and supported):

- A public API for low-level graphics access (e.g. OpenGL/Vulkan/WebGPU-like control)
- A stable and documented native texture pipeline for custom GPU renderers
- An official mechanism to embed external rendering engines (e.g. custom OpenGL/Vulkan contexts) without relying on undocumented engine behavior
- A supported plugin architecture for graphics-heavy use cases (similar to how video players use Texture widgets, but more general)

Currently, these use cases are only achievable via fragile community packages (e.g. `flutter_gl`, 'flutter_opengl', 'flutter_angle', 'openworld_gl') that frequently break with Flutter updates and require patching, which makes serious graphics applications difficult to maintain.

This proposal is not asking Flutter to include a 3D engine, but rather to provide a stable, officially supported foundation for advanced rendering use cases.

title: [native assets] Bundle code assets debug symbols in separate dsym file
body:
Currently the the dynamic libraries from build hooks are bundled as is.

On Android and iOS we should run some compiler commands to strip them from debug symbols to reduce code size and provide the symbol files on the side.

* Android: `objcopy --only-keep-debug libmylib.so libmylib.so.sym` + `strip --strip-debug --strip-unneeded libmylib.so` + `objcopy --add-gnu-debuglink=libmylib.so.sym libmylib.so`.
* iOS: `symutil libmylib.dylib` + `strip -x -S libmylib.dylib`

For iOS we have code signing logic on copying. That would probably the right place to strip the symbols as well:

https://github.com/flutter/flutter/blob/3d04e9abdb8a7662a6522a0f86ea620c0c94562f/packages/flutter_tools/lib/src/isolated/native_assets/ios/native_assets.dart#L95

For Android, we can add the stripping also just before the copying:

https://github.com/flutter/flutter/blob/3d04e9abdb8a7662a6522a0f86ea620c0c94562f/packages/flutter_tools/lib/src/isolated/native_assets/android/native_assets.dart#L19

Thanks for the suggestion @mraleph!

(Note: since the debug symbols can be stripped by the SDK invoking the build hooks, we don't provide anything the build hooks itself to allow for reporting debug symbols in a separate file.)

title: [Android] Allow renaming 'libapp.so' to avoid collision in nested AARs
body:
Body:

I am working on a complex integration scenario and have encountered a symbol/file conflict regarding libapp.so.

The Architecture:

    Module A (Flutter Module): A standard Flutter module.

    Project B (Android Native App): This is a native Android project that integrates Module A as a dependency.

    AAR Export: I am packaging Project B (which now contains Flutter) into an .aar file.

    Host Project (Flutter App): A separate Flutter project that needs to consume the .aar from Project B.

The Problem:
When I attempt to build the Host Project, the build fails due to a duplicate file conflict:
2 files found with path 'lib/arm64-v8a/libapp.so' (and other ABIs).

Both the Host Project and the integrated AAR contain a libapp.so file. Since Flutter's build engine expects a specific filename for the Dart entry point, I cannot simply rename one of them without breaking the initialization.

Questions:

    Is there a way to rename the compiled Dart library (e.g., to libapp_module.so) during the AAR generation process?

    How can I configure the FlutterEngine in the Native AAR to load a custom-named .so file?

    Are there any Gradle scripts or packagingOptions that can merge or handle these two Flutter environments without one overwriting the other?

Any guidance on how to manage multiple libapp.so files in a single Android APK would be greatly appreciated.

title: `flutter analyze` should mention when `dart fix --apply` could fix the problems
body:
When you run `flutter analyze`, if some of the errors could be autofixed by `dart fix --apply`, it should say so.

This ticket reopens #124150.

Why do I reopen it? I would like to draw attention to this feature again, as it could be a helpful improvement in the **era of coding agents**.

The output of `flutter analyze` is something like:

```
Analyzing 17 items...                                      

   info • Use 'const' with the constructor to improve performance • app/lib/calendrical_events/page/calendrical_events_page.dart:323:32 • prefer_const_constructors
   info • Use 'const' with the constructor to improve performance • app/lib/calendrical_events/page/calendrical_events_page.dart:333:32 • prefer_const_constructors
   info • Use 'const' with the constructor to improve performance • app/lib/calendrical_events/page/calendrical_events_page.dart:360:19 • prefer_const_constructors
   info • Use 'const' with the constructor to improve performance • app/lib/calendrical_events/page/calendrical_events_page.dart:363:22 • prefer_const_constructors
   info • Use 'const' with the constructor to improve performance • app/lib/calendrical_events/page/calendrical_events_page.dart:364:20 • prefer_const_constructors
   info • Use 'const' with the constructor to improve performance • app/lib/calendrical_events/page/calendrical_events_page.dart:365:18 • prefer_const_constructors
   info • Use 'const' with the constructor to improve performance • app/lib/calendrical_events/page/calendrical_events_page.dart:367:15 • prefer_const_constructors
   info • Use 'const' with the constructor to improve performance • app/lib/calendrical_events/page/calendrical_events_page.dart:368:15 • prefer_const_constructors
   info • Use 'const' with the constructor to improve performance • app/lib/calendrical_events/page/calendrical_events_page.dart:369:15 • prefer_const_constructors
   info • Use 'const' with the constructor to improve performance • app/lib/navigation/drawer/sign_out_dialogs/sign_out_dialogs.dart:49:12 • prefer_const_constructors

10 issues found. (ran in 6.5s)
```

Codex, and I assume also other coding agents would do the same, edited every single file manually instead of just running `dart fix --apply`. Editing every single file manually is slower and consumes more tokens. Mentioning `dart fix --apply` would fix this issue as a coding agent then run `dart fix --apply`. Of course, this would also be a great DX for a human developer.

**Workaround:** Mentioning in the `AGENTS.md` file that for `info` warnings the agent should first try `dart fix --apply`, then `flutter analyze` again and if the warnings persist, fix them manually.

Another way to approach this: If coding agents support LSP (https://github.com/openai/codex/issues/8745), they could also easily use the quick fix for lints.

title: test coverage broken with pub workspaces on Flutter 3.41 and Dart 3.11
body:
Flutter 3.41 requires test 1.28 due to the [pinned test_api](https://github.com/flutter/flutter/blob/44a626f4f0027bc38a46dc68aed5964b05a83c18/packages/flutter_test/pubspec.yaml#L20) version in `flutter_test`.

This means that any projects that contain a mix of pure Dart packages and Flutter packages, are using pub workspaces, and collect test coverage cannot upgrade to Flutter 3.41 and Dart 3.11.

Seems like the bug was fixed in `test: 1.29.0` but it's not possible to use with the latest stable version of Flutter due to the explicit pinning of `test_api` in `flutter_test`.

`package:test` issue: https://github.com/dart-lang/test/issues/2570

In my opinion, this should be hot fixed ASAP.  cc @Piinks, @kevmoo @mit-mit (not sure who else to tag).

title: Proposal: take advantage of `relayout` parameter in `_ScaffoldLayout` class constructor
body:
The Scaffold widget's build method creates an `AnimatedBuilder` so that the `CustomMultiChildLayout` widget is rebuilt every frame the FAB animation sends a notification. https://github.com/flutter/flutter/blob/7bafe12c4f69c41b8bee4466296e6a30ba3564ea/packages/flutter/lib/src/material/scaffold.dart#L3207-L3233
I believe we can factor out the `AnimatedBuilder` by utilizing the [MultiChildLayoutDelegate constructor](https://main-api.flutter.dev/flutter/rendering/MultiChildLayoutDelegate/MultiChildLayoutDelegate.html)'s `relayout` parameter. This would allow the FAB animation to update the layout directly, rather than triggering a build and a subsequent [shouldRelayout](https://main-api.flutter.dev/flutter/rendering/MultiChildLayoutDelegate/shouldRelayout.html) call.

title: [go_router_builder] Support custom Codec or Converter for TypedQueryParameter
Proposal - TextField for max-length validation, add option to count Unicode code points instead of grapheme clusters
body:
### Use case

In Zulip, we sometimes want to send a user-provided string to the server, and the server validates the string by counting its Unicode code points. See for example "max_stream_name_length:" in [this Zulip API doc](https://zulip.com/api/register-queue):

> The maximum allowed length for a channel name, in Unicode code points. Clients should use this property rather than hardcoding field sizes.

We'd like a simple way to validate the string client-side before passing it to the server.


### Proposal

The [`TextField` widget](https://api.flutter.dev/flutter/material/TextField-class.html) has params [`maxLength`](https://api.flutter.dev/flutter/material/TextField/maxLength.html) and [`maxLengthEnforcement`](https://api.flutter.dev/flutter/material/TextField/maxLengthEnforcement.html). Those work by counting Unicode _grapheme clusters_, not code points, so if we want to perfectly match the client-side validation/enforcement to the server, we can't use those.

I propose adding another optional param to `TextField` that configures whether `maxLength` is interpreted as grapheme clusters, which will remain the default behavior, or as code points.

title: Need an import for USB/Serial that will work across all Platforms!
body:
### Use case

Flutter currently has no official, cross-platform API for USB/Serial communication (CDC-ACM, USB UART, etc.).

Developers building hardware-connected applications (LoRa radios, microcontrollers, GNSS receivers, industrial devices, etc.) must rely on third-party plugins that:

* Vary widely in quality and maintenance
* Have inconsistent APIs across platforms
* Do not support all Flutter targets (Android, iOS, macOS, Windows, Linux, Web)
* Require platform-specific conditional code
* Expose low-level native complexity to Dart

For applications that interact with embedded hardware, serial communication is a fundamental transport similar to sockets or BLE.

---

## Problem

There is currently no:

* Official `dart:usb` or `dart:serial` API
* Unified abstraction for:

  * Device enumeration
  * Permission handling
  * Opening/closing ports
  * Configuring baud rate, parity, stop bits
  * Reading/writing streams
  * Handling hot-plug events
* Consistent behavior across:

  * Android (USB host API)
  * Windows (COM ports)
  * Linux (/dev/tty*)
  * macOS
  * Web (WebUSB / WebSerial)

This results in fragmentation and duplicated effort across the ecosystem.

---


## Why this belongs in Flutter

* Hardware-integrated mobile and desktop apps are increasingly common (IoT, amateur radio, telemetry, robotics).
* Serial communication is a fundamental transport layer.
* The ecosystem currently depends on community plugins with inconsistent support.
* An official API would significantly reduce fragmentation and improve reliability across platforms.

---

## Alternatives considered

Current third-party plugins:

* Often support only one or two platforms
* Are inconsistently maintained
* Use different APIs and behavioral semantics

A standardized implementation would benefit the broader ecosystem.


### Proposal

Introduce an official cross-platform USB/Serial abstraction in Flutter (or Dart) that:

* Provides a unified Dart API
* Uses platform channels internally
* Supports:

  * CDC-ACM / USB serial devices
  * Standard UART serial ports
  * Hot-plug detection
  * Permission APIs where required
* Aligns with existing stream-based APIs (`Stream<List<int>>`, `Sink<List<int>>`)
* Mirrors the ergonomics of `dart:io` sockets

title: test coverage broken on Flutter 3.41.2
body:
Test coverage is broken in `package:test: >1.26.3 <1.30.0` due to  https://github.com/dart-lang/test/issues/2581. This was fixed and released in `v1.30.0` of `package:test` however it's not possible to use v1.30.0 in the latest stable Flutter version due to the [pinned test_api](https://github.com/flutter/flutter/blob/90673a4eef275d1a6692c26ac80d6d746d41a73a/packages/flutter_test/pubspec.yaml#L20) version in `flutter_test`.

In my opinion, we should bump `test_api` to 0.7.10 in a hot fix.  cc @Piinks, @kevmoo @mit-mit (not sure who else to tag).

```diff
- test_api: 0.7.9
+ test_api: 0.7.10
```

title: SHA-256 checksum mismatch in release manifest for Flutter 3.41.3 (macOS, Windows)
body:
The release archives for Flutter 3.41.3 stable on macOS (x64, arm64) and Windows (x64) were replaced on the CDN on 2026-03-04, but the SHA-256 checksums in the release manifests (`releases_macos.json`, `releases_windows.json`) were not updated. The Linux manifest (`releases_linux.json`) was correctly updated.

### Reproduction

The following script checks all three platforms. Linux passes; macOS and Windows fail.

```bash
#!/usr/bin/env bash
set -euo pipefail

MANIFEST_BASE="https://storage.googleapis.com/flutter_infra_release/releases"
VERSION="3.41.3"
CHANNEL="stable"

if command -v sha256sum &>/dev/null; then
  SHA256CMD="sha256sum"
elif command -v shasum &>/dev/null; then
  SHA256CMD="shasum -a 256"
else
  echo "ERROR: sha256sum or shasum not found" >&2
  exit 1
fi

for platform in linux macos windows; do
  echo "--- Platform: $platform ---"
  manifest_url="${MANIFEST_BASE}/releases_${platform}.json"
  manifest=$(curl -sS "$manifest_url")
  base_url=$(echo "$manifest" | jq -r '.base_url')

  echo "$manifest" | jq -c \
    --arg ver "$VERSION" --arg ch "$CHANNEL" \
    '.releases[] | select(.version == $ver and .channel == $ch)' |
  while IFS= read -r entry; do
    archive=$(echo "$entry" | jq -r '.archive')
    expected=$(echo "$entry" | jq -r '.sha256')
    arch=$(echo "$entry" | jq -r '.dart_sdk_arch // "x64"')
    file_url="${base_url}/${archive}"

    echo "  Arch:            $arch"
    echo "  Manifest SHA256: $expected"
    actual=$(curl -sS -L "$file_url" | $SHA256CMD | awk '{print $1}')
    echo "  Actual SHA256:   $actual"
    if [ "$expected" = "$actual" ]; then
      echo "  Result: PASS"
    else
      echo "  Result: *** MISMATCH ***"
    fi
    echo ""
  done
done
```

Results:

```bash
--- Platform: linux ---
  Arch:            x64
  Manifest SHA256: f620731182669befdcd7fc56962def81b0434d0ba4358f363e88059f37656670
  Actual SHA256:   f620731182669befdcd7fc56962def81b0434d0ba4358f363e88059f37656670
  Result: PASS

--- Platform: macos ---
  Arch:            x64
  Manifest SHA256: 63f43c4502a5d10a4c5966b4bc2544b4f11a48d7c12f10d180f992b96c1f4e4e
  Actual SHA256:   1886e02d3242f26f1121d2cc62c4122fa311496826f0abafe7b1d68817db61b1
  Result: *** MISMATCH ***

  Arch:            arm64
  Manifest SHA256: 41077d2690f5fa5cad4f6c93e841fff201879a62184e88be39dc3974b2a7941a
  Actual SHA256:   751b21251c06c437b20c0e9f8584012c2a3efb98fa75652ca34155796110d716
  Result: *** MISMATCH ***

--- Platform: windows ---
  Arch:            x64
  Manifest SHA256: 02135acd0a8f7d5aa904e0ed257f2573cfb4bf45104a63ee9b7821a371ff9028
  Actual SHA256:   817716ec4090e17b66b0f7322b07e67caf572b6d22a3daabc08ed74fb79e49c2
  Result: *** MISMATCH ***
```

### Manifest snapshots

Retrieved at 2026-03-04 19:07 JST.

- [releases_macos_2026-03-04.json](https://github.com/user-attachments/files/25737950/releases_macos_2026-03-04.json)
